chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The right to access to his own school records, the confidentiality of which the school shall maintain and preserve, however, students shall not be deprived of viewing and re -evaluating his/her class stan ding as reflected in the class record; 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The right to the issuance of official certificates, diplomas, transcript of records, transfer credentials and other similar doc uments within 30 days from request; 6.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The right to publish a student newspaper and similar p ublications, as well as the right to invite resource persons during convocation, symposium and other activities of similar n ature; 7.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The right to free expression of opinions and suggestions, and to effective channels of communication with appropriate academi c and administrative bodies of the school inst itution; 8.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The right to form, establish, join and participate in organizations and societies recognized by the school to foster their intellectual, cultural, spiritual, and physical growth and development, or to form, establish, join and maintain organizations and societies for purposes not co ntrary to law; 9.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The right to participate in the formulation and development of policies affecting the school through representation in the appropriate 5 body/bodies of the schoo l to be dete rmined by the Governing Board; and 10.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The right to be free from involuntary contributions, except those approved by their own organiz ations or societies.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Obligations All bona fide students of the Uni versity shall be covered by all obligation s as citizens mandated in the cons titution and those stated under the Education Act of 1982 as fo llows: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To render personal military or civic service under the conditions provided for by law; 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To develop patriotism and nationalism, love of humanity, respec t for human rights and appreciation of the role of national heroes in the historical d evelopment of the country; 3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To understand the rights and accept the duties of cit izenship, strengthen his ethical and spiritual values, develop moral character and persona l discipline, critical and creative thin king; 4.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To exert his utmost to develop his potentialities for service, particularly, by pursuing an education suited to his abilities, in order that he may b ecome an asset to his family and to society; 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To uphold the ac ademic integrity of the school, endeavor to achieve excellence and abide by the rules and regulations governing his academic responsibilities and moral inte grity; 6.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To promote and maintain the peace and tranquility of the school by observing the rules of dis cipline, and by exerting efforts to a ttain harmonious relationships with fellow students, the teaching and academic staff and other perso nnel; 7.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To participate actively in civil affairs and in the promotion of the general welfare, particularly in the social, economic and cultural development of his community and in the attainment of a just, compassionate and orderly soc iety; 8.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To exercise his rights responsibly in the knowledge that he is answerable for any infringement or violation of the public welfare and the rights of ot hers; 9.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To love, respect and obey his parents, and cooperate with them to maintain the family sol idarity; 11.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To respect the customs and traditions of our people, the duly constituted authorit ies, the laws of our country and the principles of demo cracy; 12.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To help in the observance and exercise of individual rights and strengthening of freedom in the pursuit of progress, prosperity and world peace.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Responsibilities Students have responsibili ties that they accept through membership in the University‟s learning community.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Each student should approach academic endeavors, relationships, and personal responsibil ities with a strong commitment to personal integrity and mutual respect.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: As members of the academic community, students have the following respons ibilities: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To be courteous, respectful, and orderly in their behavior inside and outside the University, especially when they use the name of, or represent the Unive rsity; 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To wear the University I.D. at all times within the campus; Generally, t he university strictly implements the No ID, No Entry Policy.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the st udent tempo rarily misplaced or forgot to carry his/her ID, he/she shall present a certificate of registration (COR) to the guard before entry.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The guard shall write a remark, date and affix signature over printed name at the back of the student‟s COR.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The loss o f an ID card should be reported immediately to the Office of Student Affairs and Service s (OSAS) and the Office of the Registrar which shall authorize the release of a new ID card upon payment of t he required fee to the Cashier.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Loss of an ID shall re quire the student to present an affidavit of loss from a notary public before applying for a new ID.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the student could not apply immediately for new ID, t he affidavit of loss may be used as temporary entry to university but only for three (3) days, henc e, the student shall apply for new ID there after.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: In case of recovered ID due to loss, the OSAS shall allow the cancellation of recorded loss ID.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Tampering of ID is a grave violation of the university rule, hence, such act s hall be subjected to disciplinary s anctions of this m anual.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Once a tampered ID is discovered upon entry to university, the security personnel shall at once confiscate the tampered ID and shall accompany the student to the respective dean.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The design, style and description of uniform is per college/campus prescription.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The wearing of organization shirt is optional, hence, if a student does not wear t he organization shirt, he/she should wear the prescribed daily uniform.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: During these days, the wearing of clothes should not be provocative and must not offend the values of other member of the community.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To ensure safety among the students, a prescribed laboratory outfit must be observed during laboratory works.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Cross -dressing is not allowed, except in social occasions like College Night and S tudent s‟ Ball.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Proper haircut will depend on the prescription requ ired by the respective courses.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Cultural performers are exempted to this ruling during competitions or as may be needed.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The wearing of earrings amo ng male students is prohibited, while female students should refrain from wearing excessive jewelries for security purposes.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Student should be sensible enough to wear what is appropriate and proper in accordance to the mores, values and culture of the education community.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Obedience to the Laws of the Land As people of the Republic of the Philippines, all students of the university must respect the rights of others and shall adhere to the laws of the nation, including, but not limi ted to: A.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 7610: Special Protection of Children against Abuse, Exploitation and Discrimination Act of 1992 B.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Republic Act 8293: Protect ing the Intellectual Property Code of the Philippines of 1997 8 F.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Stud ents’ Orientation All new students are required to attend t he Handog Ni BulSU Kay Bunso.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: It is a yearly student orientation intended for new (freshmen and transfer) students of the U niversity.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Student orientation programs are designed to initiate the inte gration of newly admitted first -year and transfer undergraduate students into the academic, cultural, and social climate of the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The program is designed to prepare freshmen for the academic rigor at BulSU, introduce them to the rigors and joys of schooling traditions and acquaint them with our campus resources.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The program is an opportunity for the new st udents to discover the different academic facilities of the BSU community.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: During the program they will begin to build a network of resources that will help them to start their BSU journey on the right path.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Old students are also given an annual Re-orientation because it is expected that some of the rules and regulations are forgotten as the students stay in the university.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Admission Requirements Admission Policy Guidelines Application for admission to the Bulacan State University must be filed with the Office of Admissions.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The university typically receives more applications for admission than it can accommodate.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Admission is based on academic performance (grade point average), the BSU Admission Test scores (BSUAT), and interview results.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The applicant is required to submit the necessary documents for admission, must pay the BSUAT fee and, eventually if qualified for admission, the enrolment reservation fee.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: An applicant who graduates from the elementary level and secondary level from the Department of Education shall be eligible for admission to Junior 9 high school and to any degree programs re spectively.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: However, a student who has not completed the secondary level but who has qualified in the Philippine Educational Placement Test (PEPT) may be eligible for admission.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: An applicant of a foreign Senior high school who has not fully satisfied the specific requirements of certain degree programs may be admitted, provided however, that his/her deficiencies shall be corrected during the initial school term.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: An applicant who did not graduate from high school but completed at least eleven curriculum ye ars of elementary and secondary education in other countries may be admitted at the discretion of the university and the Office of Admissions and Orientation, provided however, that he or she takes the necessary courses to offset any noted deficiencies.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: An applicant who has completed the 12 -year curriculum from foreign countries may also be considered for admission and may be given advance credits at the discretion of the university and the Office of Admissions and Orientation.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: An applicant whose parent is a regular faculty or personnel of the University shall be entitled for admission provided however, that he/she meets the requirements for application.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Aside from the conditions set forth for admission of a foreign student into any degree courses and the policies set by the Commission on Higher Education (CHED), it must be manifested that the foreign student has the means enough to support his or her education in the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: In cognizance of the constitutional right on academic freedom, admission to the Bulacan State University is open to all students who meet the above requirements and qualifications.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Students who qualify for enrolment shall also qualify to stay in the University for the entire duration for which they are expected to complete the spec ified program of study.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: However, in the case of student delinquency, violation of the University rules and policies, sickness, and other things inimical to the academic community the student shall not be stymied for transfer to other learning institutions.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The University will not discriminate on the grounds of age, race, color, nationality, religion, gender or sexual orientation.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: However, the university reserves the right to deny admission on the basis of overall academic record and to limit enrolment by s electing from among qualified applicants those who will be admitted.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Admission is on a FIRST -COME -FIRST -SERVED basis according to the scheduled dates for the processing of credentials, availability of slots and college admission requirements.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Passer of Philippine Educational Placement Test (PEPT) with an average rating of 82% or higher in the five subject areas of the: Mathem atics, 10 Communication Arts (English), Science, Communication Arts (Filipino), and Araling Panlipunan who are qualifiers for Junior High School, or College; 4.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Passer of Alternative Learning System Accreditation & Equivalency (ALS A & E) Test for Secondary Leve l with 100 or higher Standard Score (SS) and an essay -writing proficiency level of 2 or higher; 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A Filipino or foreign national who are graduate or candidates for graduation from foreign schools with K -12 Program; and 6.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Policy for Shifters, Transferees and Examinees for Ladderized Programs For shifters (must have satisfactory grades), transferees, and students under ladderized course program, they are required to accomplish the application form availabl e at the Admissions Office and must be guided accordingly.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Shifters shall be required to take the shifter‟s examinations if they intend to change programs (e.g.; from Education to Engineering or from Engineering to Architecture).
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: However, shifters who in tend to change their majors are not required to take said test.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Shifters from external campuses shall also be required to take the shifter‟s exams.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Students on a ladderized program who may wish to continue their baccalaureate degrees will be required to ta ke the continuing exams.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Transferees from other schools who may wish to study in the University may apply for admission provided however, that they meet the qualifications set forth and that the program in which they wish to enroll is open for admission.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Must have earned a senior high school diploma from a recognized secondary school; 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Must submit fully filled –out application form with two (2) pieces of 2”x 2” color ID picture with white background ( comput er generated pictures will not be accepted ) 3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Transfer Student A student fr om a recognized institution of higher learning may apply as transfer student to the university subject to the following limitations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Must submit fully filled –out application form with two (2) pieces of 2”x 2” color ID picture with white background ( comput er generated pictures will not be accepted ) 11 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The grade point average of all the subjects taken is 2.5 or better, and without a grade of 5.0 or its equivalent in any academic subject.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The student has completed not more than fifty (50) percent of the units required for the course.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The student must submit all necessary transfer credentials like transcript of records and honorable dismissal for submission to the registrar.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any transfer student may apply for transfer credit to the Office of the Registrar, which s hall evaluate the student‟s credentials and if valid, recommend to the Vice President for Academic Affairs the grant of such credit.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Must submit fully filled –out application form with two (2) pieces of 2”x 2” color ID picture with whi te background ( computer generated pictures will not be accepted ) 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Must pay tuition and other miscellaneous fees based on the pre scribed rate/s for foreign students by the university; 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Secure application form for admission to the Office of the Admissions and Orientation after fully complying wi th all the course requirements on the scheduled period of application.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Submit o riginal copy and one (1) photocopy of;  Form 138 (report card) with 2nd grading period (for college freshman applicant)  Certificate of Registration and class cards (for shift er)  Certificate of Registration and class cards (for transferee)  Certificate of Registration and class cards (for BIT 3rd Year)  Transcript of Records (for 2nd courser)  Transcript of Records (for C.P.T.E.)  Form 138 or report card with final rating ( for Gr ade 7) 3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Submit properly filled out application form that will be issued upon the evaluation of your requirements.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Pay admission examination fee amounting to P300.00 (non -refundable) to the cashier‟s office.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Submit to the Office of Admissions and O rientation the official receipt, fully accomplished application form and other requirements for the BSUAT schedule.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: BSUAT results will be posted on the date listed in the timetable attached in your exam schedule.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The BSUAT shall be administered accor ding to the guidelines set forth by the Office of Admissions and Orientation of the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Successful applicants who passed the BSUAT and included in the shortlist issued by Office of the Admi ssions and Orientation are required to submit the original copy following documents: 9.1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Certification of good moral character duly signed b y the principal or guidance counselor of the high school last attended, with school dry seal; 9.5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Applicants who fail to qualify for the first cours e preference may qualify for other courses on condition that their grades meet the grade requirements of the college/course of their second choice.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Note:  In applying for admission, any a uthorized persons may file the application forms for the applicant, (e.g. parents, relatives, teachers, guardians).  The name indicated in the birth certificate must be the same name that appears in all admission documents.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Otherwise, a notarized Affidavit of Discrepancy must be submitted together with the credentials.  Subm ission of any falsified or tampered documents and/or concealment of information constitute grounds for disqualification.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Provisions f or Persons w ith Disabilities ( PWDs) The Bulacan State University welcomes applications from people with disabilities (PWDs ).
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: As such, BSU commits itself to adhering to RA 7277 and making reasonable adjustments to enable students to participate fully in student life.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: BulSU views applications from PWDs on the same grounds as those from other applicants that are assessed purely on academic merit and potential, according to the published selection criteria relevant to the course applied for.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: BulSU has the duty under Republic Act 7277 to provide for the rehabilitation, self -development and self -reliance of disabled persons and th eir integration into the mainstream of society and for other purposes.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: BulSU makes some adjustments in anticipation of disabled students‟ support requirements (e.g. improving physical access to buildings), but also appreciate that individuals are different and further changes may need to be considered and implemented.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Change of Academic Load Change of academic load refers to adding or changing of subjects enrolled.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any student may request to add and/or change his academic load within the first two weeks of regular classes, subject to the approval of the academic unit head concerned.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Substitution of Subjects Students may substitute a new subject for an old one under the following circumstances: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: When the old subject is under an old curriculum, which was revised or replaced by a new one, and that the subject is not offered anymore; 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The new substitute subject must have equal or more credit units than the old one.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Tutorial and Special Classes Tutorial classes are strictly given to senior level students only, that is, if the one subject being requested would mean his graduation for the particular term.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The student must secure a certification from the office of the Registrar that he/she is a gr aduating student before he/she can request for it.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The request for tutorial classes must be recommended by the College/ Campus Dean for approval of the Vice -President for Academic Affairs for the main campus and Vice President for Executive Operations for satellite campuses.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Special classes, on the other hand, may be requested by any irregular student who wishes to repeat or take the subject in advanced, provided that the subject is not offered during the current semester or may be in conflict with the student‟s class schedule.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: There must have a minimum of ten (10) students requesting for the specific subject before it can be granted by the College/Campus Dean.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The honorarium of the instructor/professor shall be charged to the requesting student.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Dro pping of Subjects Any student may officially drop his subject(s) until one (1) week before the midterm examinations as scheduled in the University calendar.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The student must file the official dropping form at the Registrar‟s Office for this purpose.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 14 Stude nt who decides to drop his subject or withdraw from his course shall be entitled to refund in accordance with the following guidelines: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If dropping of subject or withdrawal from the course is done before the scheduled opening of classes, the student is en titled to the full refund of all fees paid except registration fee; 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If dropping of subject or withdrawal from the course is done after the opening of classes, regardless of whether the student attended his classes or not, only the tuition fee shall be refu nded subject to the following conditions: a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If dropping or withdrawal is done within the first week of the opening of classes, the student shall be charged an amount equivalent to 30% of the total tuition fees for the whole term; b.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If dropping or withdrawal is done within the second week of the opening of classes, the student shall be charged an amount equivalent to 50% of the total tuition fees for the whole term; c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If dropping or withdrawal is done within the third week of the opening of classes, the student s hall be charged an amount equivalent to 70% of the total tuition fees for the whole term; d.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If dropping or withdrawal is made after the third week from the opening of classes, the student shall be charged the total amount due, and therefore he is not entitl ed to any refund.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any student who has been advised by the University physician to discontinue studies due to health reasons shall be allowed to refund the tuition fee he /she paid in full.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Only students who are included in the official lists of enrol ment provided by the Manag ement Information System (MIS) O ffice have permission to attend the class.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Sit -in student may attend the class, subject to the approval of the academic head conce rned.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A student shall be marked tardy if he arrives in the class 15 minutes after the start of the scheduled time.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A student shall be marked absent from the class if he /she arrives 20 minutes after the start of the scheduled time.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Unless the subject teacher concerned gave prior notice, students may leave their assigned room if the teacher fails to come within 15 minutes after the start of the scheduled time for a one -hour class, 30 minutes for a two -hour class, 45 minutes for a three -hour class, and so on.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The student shall be marked absent if he/she leaves their assigned room before the prescribed waiting time.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Absences Absences incurred due to any of the following reasons sha ll be excused: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Official representation in curricular, co -curricular and extra -curricular activities.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Absence(s) incurred due to any of the aforementioned reasons do not exempt concerned students from complying with the regular requirements of their course.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: An excused absence, however, should not exceed more than thirty percent (30%) of required total attendance per semester.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any student who accumulates more than twenty percent (20%) of the time of unexcused absences from any of his/her subject before the midterm examinations shall be dropped automatically from that subject.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The instructor concerned shall mark him/her FDA (failure due absences) or UD (unofficially dropped) from his subject.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Leave of Absence Any student who wishes to temporarily discontinue his studies, may be allowed provided that the maximum allowance for official leave of absence must not exceed one year.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the student exceeds the maximum l eave of absence, the dean may carry out academic penalty by requiring the student to re -enroll three to six (3-6) additional academic units.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A leaving student shall do the following procedures: Step 1: Request a Leave of Absence (LOA) Form at the Office of the Registrar.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Step 3: After completing all the requirements stated in the form, return the copy of LOA form to the Office of the Registrar.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Student may photocopy the LOA and request the receiving officer/clerk to write the complete name and affix signature on the photocopy of LOA form as proof of receipt.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A returning student shall do the following procedures: Step 1: Student must personally appear to the Office of the Regist rar and request for readmission.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The student shall personally give the slip to the dean or his authorized representative.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the returnee is on his/her penultimate year or senior year but follows an old curriculum, Part II; Letter C of this manual shall apply.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: However if the returnee is on his/her 2nd year level (for a 4 -Year course) and 3rd year level (for a 5-Year course), he/she shall follow the new curriculum.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Maximum Residency Requirement As government funded institution, the subsidy given to each student has a limited time frame so that other qualified aspiran ts may also enjoy the same privilege.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: For this reason, all students must observe the residency requirement of the university.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Generally, a ll students must complete their courses within the period prescribed in the curriculum.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Students who could not complet e the course within the period prescribed in the curriculum may be allowed to complete their course within the maximum allowable p eriod: 2.1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: For a two-year course, the maximum allowable period of completion is four (4) years; 2.2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: For a four-year course, the maximum allowable period of comple tion is six (6) years; 2.3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: For a five -year co urse, the maximum allowable period of completion is seven and a half (7½ ) years.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The rule on the maximum allowable period of completing the course exempts the student s who are on official leave of absence (LOA).
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Exemption from the rule on the maximum allowable period of completing the course may be granted to working stude nts upon recommendation by the D ean and approval of the Vice P resident for Academic Affairs.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: St udents who fail to complete their courses within the maximum allowable p eriod may be allowed to continue, provided, that the government‟s subsidy on tuition will be forfeited, and therefore the students shall pay appropriate tuition as prescribed by the fi nance office.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Schedule of regular midterm and final examinations shall be officially announced to all students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All students must present examination permit when taking the scheduled midterm and final examinations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Students must settle their financial oblig ations before they can enroll for the next semester/ term.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Administration of special/removal examinations to an individual or group of students is subject t o the recommendation of the subject teacher and approval by the unit head concerned.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Subject teachers may exempt student from taking their final examinations if the student has a pre -final grade of at least 1.5 and has complied with all the requirements o f the subject.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The academic performance of the student shall be rated using the following grading system: GRADE EQUIVALENT 1.00 97 – 100 % 1.25 94 – 96 % 1.50 91 – 93 % 1.75 88 – 90 % 2.00 85 – 87 % 2.25 82 – 84 % 2.50 79 – 81 % 2.75 76 – 78 % 3.00 75 % (Passed) 4.00 Conditional Passed 5.00 Failed Inc.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Incomplete D Officially Dropped FDA or UD Failure Due to Absences or Unofficially Dropped 4.00 (Conditional Passed ) indicates that there is a slight deficiency in the performance of the student to satisfy the minimum requirements the course for him to earn the lowest passing grade of “3”.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The student, in consultation with the subject teacher concerned, must resolve this deficiency within two (2) weeks after he/she received the grade; otherwise the grade automatically becomes “5.00”.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The grade of 4.00 shall not appear in the student‟s permanent records or transcript of records.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Inc. (Incomplete ) indicates that the student has been getting passing marks in all criteria for gr ading except that he lacks certain project or activity required in the course.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The student has a grace period of one (1) year to complete t he requirements; other wise, “Inc. ” automatically becomes “5.00”.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the stu dent got the mark of “Inc. ” in a prerequis ite subject, he may not enroll the subject in which it is a prerequisite until he has completed such prerequisite subject.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 18 D (Officially Dropped) indicates that the student officially filled a formal dropping form, duly signed by the respective instructor /professor, dean and the university registrar.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: FDA or UD (Failure Due to Absences or Unofficially Dropped) indicates that the student discontinue attending the class without notice to the teacher concerned resulting to failure or unofficially dropped the subject.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Transparency and Correction of Grades For transparency of giving fair grades, the students shall not be deprived to know the procedures used to co mpute grade and reevaluate his/ her class standing at any time.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The student should be informed o f his/her final grade before it is encoded/posted.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The student has the right to have a copy of his /her Certificate of G rades (COG) at the end of every semester/term on the scheduled date of its release.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: In exceptional cases, where an error has been committed, the instructor/professor may request authority from the Dean of his college /campus to make the nece ssary change, provided, that the supporting document is presented.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the request is grant ed, a copy of the authority from the Dean authorizing the change shall be forwarded to the Office of the Registrar for proper recording.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The faculty of each academic unit shall remedy academic delinquency by implementing the f ollowing measures and minimum standards: a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any student who obtained a failing grade in one (1) subject shall receive a warning from the d ean. b.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any student who dropped or obtained failing grades in two (2) subjects shall not be allowed to enroll the requisite subjects in the succeeding semester.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: He/she may, however, enroll minor subjects in advance, subject to the approval of the dean, provided, that he/she shall not exceed the number of units prescribed by the curriculum. c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any student who obtained failing grade s of three ( 3) subjects but less than fifty percent (50%) of his enrolled subjects shall be placed under probation for the succeeding semester, His /her load 19 shall be reduced commensurately as determined by the unit head concerned. d.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any student on probation who dropped or obtained failing grades in two (2) subjects shall be dropped from his course.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: He/s he may, however, apply to shift to another course, subject to the acceptance of the receiving Dean and approval from the Vice President for Academic Affairs. e.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any student who obtained failing grades of 50% to 75% of his/ her enrolled subjects shall be suggested shift to another course, subject to the acceptance of th e receiving Dean and approval from the Vice President for Academic Affairs. f.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any student who obtaine d a failing grades of more than 75% of his enrolled subjects shall be disqualified to continue his/her studies in the University. g.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any student on probation obtained failing grades in at least fifty percent (50%) of his enrolled subjects shall be disqualifie d to continue his/her studies in the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Note: The prescribed completion period of the course shall strictly implement to any student who will undergo the above conditions.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Gold Gear Award s The Gold Gear Awards is an annual recognition program for students for the formal awarding of academic excellence, co -curricular and extra -curricular awards.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Office of the Dean for Student Affairs and Services in coordination with the different academic units of the University shall be responsible for the conduct of this program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Academic Excellence Award These awards shall be based solely on academic excellence with the following criteria: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The President’s List Award is given to st udents wit h a grade point average of 1.20 -1.00 and no grade lower than 2.0 in any subject enrolled for the last two consecutive semesters prior to the award.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Dean’s List Award is given to students with a grade point avera ge of 1.75-1.21 and no grade lower than 2.0 in any subject enrolled for the last two consecutive semesters prior to the award.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The candidates for the awards must have been officially enrolled with a minimum of 15 academic units or as prescribed in the regular curriculum in the last two consecutive semesters, i.e., 2nd semester of the preceding academic year and 1st semester of the current academic year.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Co -Curricular and Extra -Curricular Awards To encourage excellence in co -curricular and extra - curricular activities, the University shall award non -academic honors and recognition to deserving students based on approved criteria and standards set by Committee on Awards.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Competition Winners This award shall be limited to achievements in connection with duly accredited competitions in the academic, trade skills, cultural, arts and sports to which students officially represent the university or any of its units.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The awards shall be categorized in te rms of degree/level of performance attained by the students in duly accredited competition as follows: a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Extra -Curricular Awards These special awards are given to students who made an exemplary achievements in their field of interest and unconditionally shared to BulSU community.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The candidates of these awards are nominate d by the advisers of the concerned organization and submitted to the dean of student affairs and services. a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Student Organizations Leaders Note: A candidate for any of the award shall have no disciplinary record on the current academic year.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Each student who has satisfied all the requirements of his/her course is required to apply for graduation by filing the official A pplication for Graduation form to the office of the Registrar.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All candidates for graduation must be cleared of all property and money accountabilities in the University before the commencement exercises.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: No student shall graduate from this University unle ss he/she has completed at least one year of residence prior to the date of graduation.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All disciplinary charges against a student must be resolved and sanctions completed before a student is eligible to graduate.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The comme ncement exercises for each of the academic units of the University shall be held on the dates as may be approved by the Academic Council.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All candidates for graduation from each academic unit shall be enjoined to attend their respective graduation exerc ises.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All candidates for graduation shall be required to wear the academic costume prescribed for the program during the traditional baccalaureate services and commencement exercises.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Academic Honors Academic honors shall be given to graduating student based on their grade point average and type of course as follows: Grade Point Average Degree Courses Non-degree Courses 1.00 to 1.20 Summa Cum Laude With Highest Honors 1.21 to 1.45 Magna Cum Laude With High Honors 1.46 to 1.75 Cum Laude With Honors To qualify for honors, graduating students must satisfy the following requirements: a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Completed at least seventy -five percent (75%) of the total n umber of academic units required for the course and residency of at least two years in the University; c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Enrolled at least fifteen (15) credit units prescribed in the curriculum in each of the terms or semesters.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Exemption to this rule may be allowed if: (i) the required units prescribed in the curriculum in the given term/semester are less than this minimum requirement, (ii) the subject(s) needed to meet this minimum requirement are not offered.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Outstanding Student Awards There shall be one student awardee per program for each college or campus subject to the following criteria: a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A grade point average of at least 1.75 with no grade lower than 2.0 in any subject; b.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Shall have a minimum of 15 units enrolled during the semester or as prescribed in th e regular curriculum; c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Involvement in either university or college/campus based student 22 organizations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The award shall be based on the performance in the specialization with no grade lower than 2.0 in all major subjects or as the college selection committee may deem appropriate. c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The award ee shall be selected by a special committee designated for the purpose composed mainly of teachers in the major subjects and approved by the dean of the college/satellite campus.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The candidate for the award must have been officially enrolled with a minimum of 15 academic units or as prescribed in the regular curriculum in the last two consecutive semesters without any dropped, incomplete or failing grades; c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The candidate for the award must be nominated by the adviser of the concerned organization.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The documents submitted by the nominee shall be reviewed and evaluated by the College Selection Co mmittee, The committee shall recommended the candidate to the college/campus dean for approval. d.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The candidate for the award must have a track record based on the following criteria: d.1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Length of service as elected officer of student government, recognized university or college -based organizations; d.2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Projects conceptualized and implemented while in the performance of duty as elected officer; d.3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: University Leadersh ip Award There shall be one University Leadership Award for the entire studentry.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: It is given only to a graduating student either from student government, student organizations, cultural groups or student publications.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To qualify for this award, any gra duating student/ candidate shall submit a leadership folio to the Dean of Student Affairs and S ervices, who, shall form a committee to evaluate the candidate‟s folio based on meritorious leadership component and its impact to BulSU community.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Honorary Award An honorary award is given to Student Regent upon expiration of his/her term.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Loyalty Award The Loyalty Award is given to graduating student who started Grade 7 i n BulSU and had completed his/her baccalaureate degree in the same university.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Provides basic academic information on curricular programs, general registrati on requirements and procedures; 3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Issues tr anscript of records, diploma, transfer credentials, and official certifications.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any student who has settled all his financial obligations and accountabilities in the University may secure copy of his official transcript of records, official certifications, credentials or other school records.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To secure the official transcript of records the student must file a clearance form together with official receipt of payment to the Registrar‟s office.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To effect a change in the name on the records of female student who got married while in the University, she must submit an affidavit of change of status and a copy of the marriage contract from the National Statistics Office to the Registrar‟s office.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any student who wishes to transfer to another school must submit duly accomplished and signed clearance form to the Registrar‟s Office to secure transfer credentials.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any student who is dismissed from the University shall be issued transfer credentials provided he i s cleared of all financial obligations and accountabilities in the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 24 PART III STUDENT CONDUCT AND DISCIPLINE As it pursues its vision and mission, the University places great stress on the values of respect for the dignity and worth of indiv iduals and the development of character.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Thus, it strives for an environment that promotes these va lues and believes that each student, as a member of the academic community, contributes to uphold them.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Therefore, the University clearly presents st udent ri ghts and responsibilities and establishes the disciplinary procedures to ensure that all members of the University community benefit from the promotion of these values.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: General Policies In the implementation of the rules and regulations on student conduct and discipline, the Un iversity is guided by the following policies: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All matters pertaining to student conduct and discipline shall be governed by the pertinent provisions of the University Code, the Education Act of 1982 and other general and speci al laws in relation to education.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Following the principle of en loco parentis, the University has the right to act in the best interests of the students as it sees fit through all persons charged with the supervision of st udents, which shall be deemed per sons in authority and should therefore, be accorded due respect and protection (Art.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All University officials, faculty members, staff and security pe rsonnel are authorized to enforce and request compliance to all University rules and regul ations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The investigation and disposition of student disciplinary cases shall follow the proceedings set forth in this se ction of the Handbook.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To be subjected to any disciplinary penalty only after the requirements of due process shall have been fully complied with; b.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To be penalized only on the basis of substantial evidence, the burden of proof being with the pers on filing the charge; c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To be penalized only on the basis of evidence presented and of which the respondent had been properly notified and given the opportunity to r ebut; d.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To defend himself personally or by a counsel/representative of his own choice; e.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Pending final dec ision on the charges, to enjoy all rights and privileges of a student, unless a preventive suspension has been 25 promu lgated against him.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Norms of Conduct The University believes in a happy and peaceful campus environment cond ucive to learnin g: hence, it expects that every student must, at all times exert efforts to obey the laws of the land, observe campus rules and reg ulations and follow the accepted norms of good social behavior and right conduct.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All students must strive to exemplify good moral character and act in accordance with the moral values and att itudes: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Disciplinary Sanctions and Penalties In order to maintain harmony and to protect its educational purposes and processes, the University has the authority to impose penalties or sanctio ns to any student found to have violated its rules, policies and regulations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: While disciplinary sanctions generally are commensurate with the seriou sness of the offense, multiple or repeated violations may justify more severe disciplinary sanctions.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Possible pe nalties and sanctions to be applied in various situations include: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A reprimand may be in the form of verbal counsel to student in regards to his /her negative behavior or a written reprimand describing a student‟s offense or misconduct, which places on r ecord that a student, in a specific instance, did not meet the behavioral standards expecte d at the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A written reprimand from the Dean for Student Affairs and Services or de signee to the student serves as a warning that continued conduct of the type described in the reprimand or other misconduct may result in more severe disciplinary sanction against the st udent.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: This penalty is 26 imposed upon a student who fails to pay a debt owed to the University or who has a disciplinary case pending f inal disposition.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: This penalty terminates upon paym ent of the debt or upon final disposition of the case.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A time -specific suspension is a temporary cessation of educational services and exclusion from the University property and facilities.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Board of Student Discipline shall determine the duration of suspension and shall notify the concerned offices and units of the students ser ving the penalty of suspension duly noted by the office of the University President.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Dismissal is termination of educational services and exclusion from t he University property and facilities for not less than one year.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The conditions for readmi ssion, if any, will be stated in the order of dismissal.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A dismissed student can be reinstated on ly upon the favorable action by the President or designee on his petition for reinstat ement.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Expulsion is permanent severance from the University without provision for readmi ssion.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Expulsion prohibits the student from using or entering any Uni versity property and from atten ding any University function.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If a student is expelled, the Board of Student Discipline shall notify the concerned offices and units and the Registrar, which shall have the appropriate disciplinary notation placed on the student's official transcript of records.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: This sanction is to create a constructive activity that is assigned to students as a redemptive sanction.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: It is designed t o enhance a student‟s ability to learn from the disciplinary experience and promote learning that will lead to responsible decision making in the future.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: This experience is done during vacant period of the student or as prescribed by the dean, provided the student will not affect his/her classes.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Penalties or sanctions of at least suspension for one semester shall be reflected in the student‟s permanent record.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: However, a t any time after three years from the date of the finding in which a sanction was impo sed, a student or former student may petition the University President or desi gnee to have his or her disciplinary record expunged.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The decision to expunge shall be based on the severity of the violation(s), the person‟s disciplinary record as a whole, and evidence of good behavior since the viol ation(s).
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the record is expunged any notation of a disciplinary suspension will be removed from the official academic record maintained by the O ffice of the Registrar.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Grounds for Disciplinary Sanctions The acts of misconduct and the corresponding disciplinary sanctions that may be imposed include, but are not limited to, the following: A.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Littering or distribution of unauthorized printed materials on Unive rsity campuses and premises; 1st Offense Verbal Reprimand 2nd Offense Written reprimand wi th warning; and 4 hours of Transform ational Experience.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 3rd Offense 10 hours of Transformational Experience; and Guidance I nterven tion P rogram.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Vandalism or unauthorized posting of printed materials on Unive rsity property, campuses and premises; 1st Offense Verbal Reprimand; Cleaning the walls; and Commitment letter 2nd Offense Written reprimand with warning; Cleaning the walls; and 4 hours of Transformational Experience.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 3rd Offense 10 hours of Transformational Experience; Cleaning the walls; and Guidance intervention program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Disturbance or disruption of the educational environment, classes or any education related programs or activities; 1st Offense Verbal Reprimand.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 2nd Offense Written reprimand with warning; and 4 hours of Transformational Experience.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 3rd Offense 10 hours of Transformational Experience; and Guidance intervention program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Unauthorized solicitation of funds or selling of any ticket; 1st Offense Verbal Reprimand and R estitution.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 2nd Offense Written reprimand with warning; 4 hours of Transformational Experience; and Restitution.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 3rd Offense 10 hours of Transformational Experience; Guidance intervention program; and Restitution.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Smoking, gambling or being under the influence of alcohol within the university premises; 1st Offense Written reprimand with warning; 4 hours of Transformational Experience; and Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean.

chunks.content: 2nd Offense 10 hours of Transformati onal Experience; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program.

chunks.content: 3rd Offense 20 hours of Transformational Experience; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Malicious or unfounded accus ation towards any member of the academic comm unity; 1st Offense Written reprimand with warning; 4 hours Transformational Experience; and Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 2nd Offense 10 hours Transformational Experience; Parents/guardians dialog ue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Deception, Impersonation, or F raud; 1st Offense Written reprimand wi th warning; 4 hours Transformational Experience; and Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 2nd Offense 10 hours of Transformational Experience; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Disrespectful behavior in words and in deeds or refusal to comply with directions of the University officials and e mployees acting in the performance o f their duties; 1st Offense Written reprimand with warning; 8 hours of Transformational Experience; and Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 2nd Offense 16 hours of Transformational Experience; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 29 3rd Offense 32 hours of Transformational Experience; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Damage or unauthorized presence in or use of Un iversity premises, facilities or property, in violat ion of posted signs, when closed, or after normal ope rating hours; 1st Offense Written reprimand with warning; 8 hours of Transformational Experience; Parents/guardians dialogue; and Restitution.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 2nd Offense 16 hours of Transformational Experience; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean, and Guidance intervention program; and Restitution.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 3rd Offense 32 hours of Transformational Experience; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program; and Restitution.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: For Items 1 -9: Any student who commits 4th of the same violation shall be subjected to suspension for two weeks.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Theft, attempted theft, and/or unauthorized possession or use of property/services belonging to the University or a member of the Universi ty co mmunity; 1st Offense Written reprimand with warning; 16 hours of Transformational Experience; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program; and Restitution.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 2nd Offense 1 week Suspension; 16 hours of Transformationa l Experience; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program; and Restitution.

chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Indecency in any form of obscene or lewd behavior (necking, pe tting or torrid kissing or other sexual act ) inside the university premises; 1st Offense Written reprimand with warning; 16 hours of Transformational Experience; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 2nd Offense 1 week Suspension; 16 hours of Transformational Experience; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program.

chunks.content: 3rd Offense 2 weeks Suspe nsion; 20 hours of Transformational Experience; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: For Items 10 -11: Any student who commits 4th of the same violation shall be subjected to dismissal.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Physical/verbal/sexual/me ntal/emotional abuse, threat, harassment, cyber bullying, hazing, coercion and/or other conduct that threatens or endangers the health or safety of any person; 1st Offense 2 weeks Suspension; 16 hours of Transformational Experience; Parents/guardians dial ogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 2nd Offense 1 month Suspension; 32 hours of Transformational Experience; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: For Item 12: Acts that r esulted to serious physical injuries or death shall be grounds for Expulsion even at first vi olation.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Possession, use, sale or purchase of any illegal drugs inside the university pr emises; 1st Offense Turnover to Municipal/ City Police Station; 1 month Suspension; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 2nd Offense Turnover to Municipal/ City Police Station; 1 Semester Suspension; Parents/guard ians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Carrying of firearms and other weapons within the University campuses and pre mises; 1st Offense Turnover to Municipal/ City Police Station; 1 month Suspensio n; Parents/guardians dialogue with the dean; and Guidance intervention program.
