chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Each academic unit and satellite campus of the University shall have its own Student Discipline Committee which shall be composed of the following: College Secretaries of the academic unit or satellite campus, or the Principal of the Laboratory High School as the chair, the President of the local faculty association, student governor, president of local PTA, an arbiter and a faculty member to serve as secretary.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The arbiter, preferably a lawyer or one with some legal background, shall be designated by the college dean or campus administrator, and may serve in more than one Committee.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The arbiter shall be the alternate of the Chairman, in which case the arbiter shall then have the right to vote.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the student governor is respondent or if a regular Committee member cannot attend the meeting, the University President shall de signate the alternate to attend the session.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The respective Student Discipline Committee of the academic units and satellite campuses shall have jurisdiction over -all formal complaints filed by anyone against any of their student or group of students, except those under the jurisdiction of the Committee on Student Publications, for violating any of the University rules and regulations which are subject to di sciplinary action.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Committee shall exercise the powers and perform the functions as follows: a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To receive and act on the complaints filed against any student or group of students for committing any act that is a ground for disciplinary action; b.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To conduct formal investigation and hearing or summary proceedings on all co mplaints filed against any student or group of students; c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To submit the result of formal hearing or summary proceedings and recommendations for action to the Board of Student Disc ipline for review and confirmation; 33 d.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To keep the records of case s and submit reports of their accomplishment as may be required.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Board of Student Discipline shall be composed of the following: the Dean for Student Affairs and Services as Chair, the dean of the college or satellite campus of the parties concerned, Student Government President or his representative, a legal counsel, and a secretary.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Board of Student Discipline shall review all recommendations o f the Student Discipline Committee on all cases of students involving any of the grounds for disciplinary actions.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To review the records of the case forward ed to it by the Student Discipline Committee; b.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Perform its appellate function and as such, shall affirm or reverse the decision of the Student Discipline Committee. c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To reduce or increase penalty recommended by the Student Discipline Committee as it d eems proper and just; and d.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To render a report of its acc omplishments to the University President and other appropriate bodies.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Disciplinary Proceedings Any person may file a complaint against a student or group of students who violate University po licies, rules, regulations, standards, or pr ocedures.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Formal Hearing Procedure The following rules and pr ocedures shall govern the formal hearing: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The complainant shall submit to the Student Discipline Committee written sworn statements covering his t estimony and those of his witnesses together with his documentary evidence.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If, based on such statements, a prima facie case does not exist the Student Discipline Committee shall recommend the di smissal of the case.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If, on the other hand a prima facie cas e exists, the Student Discipline Committee shall n otify the respondent in writing of the charges against him, to which shall be attached copy of the complaint and other documents submi tted.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The respondent shall be given five (5) working days after receipt of the complaint to answer the charges in writing under oath, together with supporting documents.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The hearing shall be held as much as possible within five (5) working days from submissio n of an answer or from the expiration of the period for such submi ssion.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Student Discipline Committee may, whether or not an answer is filed, thereafter call the parties for a hearing if there are facts and i ssues to be clarified from a party or witn ess.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The hearing shall be non litigious and shall be for clar ification and fact -finding.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The parties can be present at the said hearing but without the right to exa mine or cross examine.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: They may, however, submit to the Student Discipline Committee questio ns which may be asked to the party or witness concerned.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Other than the members of the Student Discipline Committee, attendance at the hearing may include the complainant, the respondent, witness, legal counsel for the parties concerned, if availed of, a nd security personnel when deemed appropr iate.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The direct evidence for the complainant and the respondent shall consists of the submitted testimonies, sworn statements and other supporting documents, without prejudice to the presentation of additional rele vant ev idence deemed necessary but was unavailable at the time of the filing of the pleadings.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All information and evidence offered or received at the hearing shall be fully disclosed to the individual(s) or group charged.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The disciplinary process is an administrative and not a criminal proceeding, therefore, there is no need to define prohibited co nduct or the hearing procedures with the specificity required in criminal law.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The hear ing shall be conducted only for purposes of ascertaining the truth and may not necessarily adhere to technical rules applicable in judicial proceedings.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The complai nant or responde nt may elect to have an attorney present at his own e xpense.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The failure of a respo ndent or representative to appear shall not be taken as an admission of guilt.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Student Discipline Committee may proceed in the absence of any party who has r eceived due notice of the hearing.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A majority of the Student Discipline Committee members shall decide on the penalty to be recommended.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The decision rendered shall be in writing, stating the grounds for which the disciplinary penalty is recommended.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The recommend ation of the Student Discipline Committee shall be submitted to the Board of Student Discipline in writing within five (5) working days after it was decided and copies shall be provided to all the parties concerned.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Immediately after receipt of the recom mendation of the Student Discipline Committee, the Board of Student Discipline shall convene 35 and render a decision within five (5) working days from the start of the review.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any party in the case may file a motion for reconsideration with the Board of Stud ent Discipline, stating the grounds for the motion, within ten (10) working days upon receipt of the decision of the Student Discipline Committee; otherwise the recommendation of Student Discipline Committee, subject to the modification of the Board of Student Discipline, shall be deemed final and executory.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the motion for reco nsideration is denied by the Board of Student Discipline and e xcept when the penalty is dismissal or expulsion, its decision shall be final and executory.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The written decision of the Board of Student Discipline shall be forwarded to the University President not later than three (3) working days after the date when the decision was rendered.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Board of Regents and all parties concerned shall be provided a copy of the decision.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the penalty is dismissal or expulsion, the concerned party may a ppeal to the University President or the Board of Regents within ten (10) working days from receipt of the denial of the motion for reconsideration.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Board of Regents, or the University President as authorized by the BOR, shall issue the Order of Execution of its decision on the case.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Summary Proceedings Summary proceeding is an alternative form of legal proceedings for cases that require prompt action without the need for formal heari ng.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: When the offender is caught in the act by any person in authority or his agents; 3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: When th e respondent is a habitual offender, that is, he has been punished for at least three (3) times for the same or different o ffenses.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Cases for summary proceedings should be brought to the Committee on Student Discipline for decision.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any recommendation rend ered as a result of summary proceedings shall be in writing stating the grounds for which the disciplinary penalty is recommended and submitted to the Board of Student Discipline for approval.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The action of the Board on the recommendation of the Committee shall be communicated in writing within five (5) working days to the respondent and forwarded to the University Pres ident.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The respondent may file a motion for reconsideration with the Board of Student Discipline stating the grounds for the motion withi n ten (10) working days upon receipt of the decision, otherwise and e xcept when the penalty is dismissal or expulsion, the decision of Board of Student Discipline shall b ecome final and executory.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 36 If the motion for reconsideration is denied by the Board, the respondent may appeal to the University President within ten (10) working days from receipt of the denial of the motion for r econsideration.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Board of Regents, or the University President as authorized by the BOR, shall issue the Order of Execution of the decision.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Student Grievance Intervention Scope and Limitation A student may seek to resolve any grievance if he/ she believes that a member of the University community has offended and/or violated his or her rights.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: This person filing the grie vance must be the alleged victim of unfair treatment; a grievance cannot be filed on behalf of another person.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Director for Student Welfare (DSW) serves as the receiver and facilitator of complaints an d grievances from students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: He/s he has neither recommendatory nor disciplinary authority, but undertakes follow -ups on complaints and grievances referred to it.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The office screens and studies the complaints and determines whether they are valid or not before they are forwarded to the office concerned for appropriate actions.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If there is a possibility of mediation, the office may make representations with the office concerned in order to settle amicably the complainant or grievance.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the complaint is directed against the DSW the student may directly report to the Dean of Student Affairs and Services who shall then take the appropriate action.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Informal Resolution Prior to invoking the formal procedures in resolving a grievance, the student is strongly encouraged, but is not required, to discuss his or her grievance with the person alleged to have caused the grievance.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The discussion should be held as soon as the student becomes aware of the act or condition that is the basis of the grievance.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The student may express his/her grievance in writing address ed directly to the person alleged to have caused the grievance.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The person alleged to have caused the grievance must respond to the student promptly either through a dialogue or through a written response.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Formal Procedure for Complaint Processing A complaint is brought before the DSW either by the Student Government office of the student alleging misconduct or infraction, abuse of authority, poor instruction or service, or any valid cause for complaint against a fellow student, a member of the facult y, a staff, or the administration and in cases when a student‟s grievance was not satisfied nor fairly responded by an informal resolution.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the complaint is valid, the DSW shall make representations with the office concerned and endorse the complaint to the said office for appropriate action.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Once the complaint is brought to the proper office, the DSW makes a series of follow -ups to ensure that the action is done.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If no action is taken by the office concer ned, the DSW may bring up the matter to the higher office, this time, with an added complaint of inaction by the office concerned.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the same inaction is present in said higher office, the DSW brings up the matter to even higher offices with added complai nts of inaction until the same reaches the University President.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: PART IV STUDENT AFFAIRS AND SERVICES VISION The Office of the Student Affairs and Services (OSAS) will be known for its transparency and professionalism in providing quality programs and s ervices conducive to the establishment of a student -centered campus environment that recognizes and respects the uniqueness of each individual student and promotes the acquisition of positive values of integrity, high ethical standards and an appreciation of life -long learning experiences.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: MISSION The Office of Student Aff airs and Services (OSAS) of Bulacan State University shall provide a full range of programs and services that assist students in identifying, clarifying and achieving their personal, edu cational, and career goals.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: GOALS The following goals form the core of responsibilities of the Office of the Student Affairs and Services (OSAS) and all its functional offices:  Promote the vision and mission of the University.  Deliver services that refle ct commitment to quality and excellence.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content:  Serve as student advocates.  Develop and cultivate active and productive relationships with faculty and other members of the academic community to further enhance the scope and depth of the student experience.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 38  Enhanc e the visibility of the offices in student affairs and services on the campus and in the community.  Advance a value of teamwork, cooperation, and collaboration within and among the offices in the student affairs and services.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Promote access to quality, relevant and effective student affairs and services 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Implement Student Welfare Program and Institutional Student Programs and services needed to ensure students‟ holistic well -being; 3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Prepare and support Student Development Programs and Services for the exploration and enhancement of students‟ full potentials for maximum involvement in nation building; 4.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Produce citizens imbued with social responsibility and accountability who can pro vide leadership for the good of the country and humanity.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: THE OFFICE OF THE DEAN FOR STUDENT AFFAIRS AND SERVICES This Office of Student Affairs and Services provides services and programs in the University that are concerned with academic support experiences of students to attain holistic student development.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Office has a multifunctional works, including, but not limited to: Student Services; Student Welfare; Student Development; Student Discipline; and Student Gov ernment affairs.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: STUDENT SERVICES This office of Student Services serves as the working arm of the Office of the Student Affairs and Services (OSAS) in implementing efficient and effective services through the different units provided by the university.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The functions of the Dire ctor for Student Services include p lanning, organizing, monitoring and evaluating the performance of units in collaboration with the student services units.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Additionally, the office provides assistance to the office of the Student Affairs and Services that may be deemed necessary for the delivery of quality services to the students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Assist the Dean of Student Affairs and Services (SAS) in planning, preparation, and implementation of student servi ces programs and activities; 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Design programs that provide quality basic services that will ensure the basic needs of the students; 3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Direct, monitor and evaluate regularly the services and programs of the Admissions and Orientation office, Student Scholarshi p and Financial Assistance Office and Students‟ Socio -community Involvement Office; 4.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Formulate policy on Student Services in collaboration with the student services heads for better up -keeping of student services program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Attend to the daily concerns and is sues of the different heads of the student services units.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Coordinate to all academic heads the other needs of students which are relevant to student services unit.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Oversee the integration of variou s programs of student services units that will enrich the academic and community life of all students; 9.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Exercise general supervision over the various Student Services units and oversee the implementation of Student Services programs; 10.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Evaluate all phases of Student Services‟ programs, and submit reports on these programs to the Dean of SAS; 11.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Report directly to the Dean of SAS all concerns that require immediate attention.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Hold co -chairmanship to various committees that are related to student services; and 13.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Perform other related functions as may be directed by the Office of the Student Affairs and Services.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: ADMISSIONS AND ORIENTATION As the gateway to the University, the Admissions and Orientation Office sees to it that thousands of applicants who wish to s tudy in BulSU are properly screened and qualified for admission, enrolled in courses which match their academic performance and potential.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The BulSU Admission Test (BSUAT) ensures that qualifiers meet the stringent admission standards of the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The office also prepares program for the orientation of the freshmen students of the university prior to the opening of classes on the first semester of every year.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Re-orientation to old students are also provided by the office during the first semester.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Objectives of the Office: In support of fair admissions, the Admissions and Orientatio n Office strives to: 40 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Ensure that admissions decisions are based on an applicant's achievements and potential; 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Implement fair admissions processes both centrally and acros s the college to ensure equality of educational opportunity regardless of the social background of applicants and conform to the applicants with special needs as provided in R.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Promote admissions processes that enable fair and equitable treatment of each individual applicant without either direct or indirect discrimination; 4.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Guarantee that admissions decisions are consistent and that each stage of the admissions process is carried out with honesty and integrity by appropriate staff with relevant a nd up -to-date knowledge and expertise; 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Create parity of esteem between and among applicants and fully recognize a wide range of international, access -based, and vocationally -related qualifications, and other indicators of potential oper ation of the admiss ions process, and 6.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Implement an approved university policy on admissions for student applicants with consideration/reasonable accommodation for those applicants and/or persons with disabilities as stipulated in R.A.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Disseminate widely the policy/guidelines of the university on student admissions to properly inform the student applicants or sta keholders.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Maintain updated records of student applicants every academic year to utilize for specific purpose.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Provide information to student applicants pertinent to basic fees collected on enrolment like tuition and other fees.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Review all the stu dents‟ applications and refer the students to financial services personnel if required.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Study, innovate, propose recruitment and admissions scheme periodically to improve admission services.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Plan and implement a regular comprehensive orientation prog ram for new and continuing students responsive to their needs, including orientation on the dynamics and nature of persons with disabilities, and relevant laws and policies affecting persons with disabilities.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Perform other duties and responsibilities a s may be deemed necessary in his/her position as Head of Admissions and Orientation Services.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: SCHOLARSHIPS AND FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE The O ffice of Scholarship and Financial Assistance is the primary venue of the university to help undergraduate studen ts reach the goal of graduation by providing scholarships, grants and financial assistance services to intellectually 41 qualified, under privileged, indigent but deserving students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The office classifies the difference between scholarships, grants and financ ial assistance such as: Scholarships are awarded based on specific criteria for students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: It is necessary that the awardees should maintain and manifest consistent excellent academic standing to uphold to the scholarship.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Grants are usually conditional upon certain qualifications as to the purpose, upholding of specified standards, or a proportional contribution by the grantee or other grantor(s).A grant or payment made to support a student‟s education, awarded on the basis of academic or other achievement such as sports, cultural inclination and arts.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Financial Assistance or Financial Aids are given by a company or an organization for the purchase of its shares or those of its holding companies.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: It is in compliance with the company‟s corporate social responsibility like: Funding of science, the provision of financing for scientific research projects.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Welfare, financial aid by (primarily) governmental institutions or charitable organizations to individuals in need a Subsidy /Student financial aid funding intended to help students pay educational expenses.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Helps provide access to quality education to poor but deserving students through a continuing scholarship program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Prepares and implements annual work plans for testing and screening of stude nt-applicants for the scholarship grants and financial assistance.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Coordinates with public/ agencies, private entities and individuals and non governmental association (NGO‟s) for additional scholarship/ financial assistance opportunities for the students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Develops, in coordination with the donors, the criteria and procedures for the selection of scholars and grantees for the various grants and scholarship slots; 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Coordinates with the Office of Admissions and the Guidance and Counseling Services Center in t he screening and selection of scholars and student -grantees in the various academic programs and courses; 6.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Publishes information on various scholarship grants and financial assistance available for the students; 7.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Coordinates with the accounting office in pro viding control mechanism over the inflow and outflow of financial assistance for students; 8.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Keeps an up -to-date list of scholars and grantees and regularly checks on their academic performance to ensure that assistance is given to appropriate recipients.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 42 Services Offered: The office issue renewal forms to student grantees provided they are still qualified and have maintained their grades during the period of their scholarship.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The students may apply on any type of scholarships and grants provided they qua lify and pass the examination in any given semester.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The following are types of scholarships, grants and financial aids categorized based on their specified requirements.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Institutional Scholarship This type of scholarship is either full (100%) or part ial (50%) tuition fee discount for one semester.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: This may be availed of by qualified freshmen students who graduated from high school as valedictorians or salutatorians as well as by those students who, during their stay in the University, have demonstrate d good academic performance subject to the following conditions: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Valedictorians and Salutatorians may enjoy full and partial scholarship grants respectively during their first semester provided that the students are: a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Members of the graduating class of at least 100 students as certified by the school principal 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Full Institutional S cholarship may be availed of by students who obtain a GPA of at least 1.50 with no grade lower than 2.0 in all of his subjects, which must be at least 15 academic units, during the preceding two semesters.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: This privilege is also given to the elected President of the Student Government during his incumbency.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Partial Institutional S cholarship may be availed of by stude nts who obtain a GPA of at least 1.75 with no grade lower than 2.0 in all of his subjects, which must be at least 15 academic units, during the preceding two semesters.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The elected Vice President of the Student Government may also avail of partial tuition fee discount during his incumbency, provided he/she does not avail the full institutional scholarship.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: BulSU Employee Dependent – receives 100% discount on tuition fee (should not incur failing mark in any subject enrolled), are asked to submit previous se mester‟s COG, parent‟s ID and birth certificate; 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Talents – those who pass and qualify for the following talent groups of the university like; Hyper -Dynamics, Band members, Lahing Kayumanggi, Saring Himig, Euphonistas – depending on their ad viser‟s recommen dation, are granted 50 -100% discount on their tuition fees; 6.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Varsity - depending on their adviser‟s recommendation, are granted 50 100% discount on their tuition fees; II.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Non -Institutional Scholarship Non-Institutional Scholarship are given by GOs, NGOs, Private Companies and other granting institutions.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Barangay Scholarships As provided for in the pertinent supervisions of the Local Government Code (R.A 7160), the University offers Barangay Scholar ship to qualified students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Sons and daughters of elective Barangay officials – are granted 100% discount on their tuition fee and are asked to submit certification from the DILG of the parent‟s post and duration and their latest COG.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: STUDENT WELFARE The Office of Student Welfare Services pertain to the provision of basic services and programs that guarantee relevant, efficient and effective support and assistance to the well -being of all students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Under the aegis of the Office of the Student Affairs and Services (OSAS), the Office of the Student Welfare (OSW) is responsible fo r the enrichment of the various programs on guidance and counseling, career development and student internship, scholarship and financial assistance, and student housing integral to the academic and community life of all students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The OSW is overseen by a director whose main function is to deliver quality services to all students through: (1) planning and implementation of requisite policies on student welfare programs and activities; (2) monitoring and evaluating the performance of specialized offices wit hin the cluster of OSW; and (3) collaborating with the heads of these specialized offices.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Assist the Dean of Student Affairs and Services (SAS) in the planning, preparation, and implementation of student welfare program and activities.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Design programs that provide quality basic services that will ensure the well-being of students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Direct, monitor and evaluate regularly the services and program s of the Guidance and Counselling Services, Career Deve lopment and Internship Services and Placement and Public Employment Office.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Formulate policy on Student Welfare in collaboration with the student welfare heads for better up -keeping of student welfare program.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Attend to the daily concerns and issues of the different heads of the student welfare units.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Coordinate to all academic heads the other needs of students which are relevant to student welfare.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Oversee the integration of various programs of student we lfare units that will enrich the academic and community life of all students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Hold co -chairmanship to various committees that are related to student welfare.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: GUIDANCE AND COUNSELING SERVICES The BulSU Guidance and Co unseling Services (GCS ) as an integral part of its educational system, opens its avenues through services for individuals, families or groups who are referred and/ or voluntarily seeking for Guidance and Counseling and all other programs it is catering.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To function effectively in his/her environment, each individual must be able to make intelligent decisions and to make adjustments to the differen t aspects of his personal life in particular, and his community in general.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: These abilities, however, are not innate rather they have to be developed gradually through the passage of time.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Because this is a fact, edu cation, then, provides numerous opportunities and learning experiences for the students‟ development of such abilities.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Hence, the Guidance and Counseling Services Center (GCS ) of the University, stands to be --one of the most important sources of such opp ortunities and experiences.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: This office recognizes each individual‟s democratic right to make choices of his/her own, although, it also do so.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: This aims to consistently adhere to its main purpose of assisting individuals in his/her decision making in such a way, as to promote the gradual development of the ability in making the right choices in life independently, without being unduly influenced by others.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Its principle adheres on integrating the student‟s knowledge of himself, his strengths and weaknesses, aptitudes, talents and interests and potentials of him and of others --for further enhancement of their roles both in his personal life, and his environments wherein he/she interacts.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Thus, GCS helps bring into focus the needs and problems of students, anticipates and uses this information to the development of curriculum in the formulation of school policies and regulations in its academic contribution and mandate.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Functions of the Office: This office provides the following set of services: Guidance ser vice by using an integrated approach to the development of well -functioning individuals primarily by helping them to utilize their potentials to the fullest; Counseling through individual and/or group intervention designated to facilitate positive change i n student behavior, feelings, and attitudes.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Appraisal by gathering information about students through the use of psychological te sts and/ or projective technique; Follow -up through systematic monitoring to determine the effectiveness of guidance activitie s; and Referral with multi -disciplinary team of specialists to ensure that special needs of students are met.

chunks.content: CAREER DEVELOPMENT AND STUDENT INTERNSHIP SERVICES The goal of current restructuring and reform initiatives of Bulacan State University is to prepare students with these important prerequisites.

chunks.content: Thus, the Career Development & Student Internship ( CarDSI) Office based on CMO 09, s2013 of the Enhanced Policies and Guidelines on Student Affairs and Services, Article VII Student Welfare which mandates that basic services such as the Career and Job Placement is necessary for the well -being of students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Since, t he University has its own Placement and Public Em ployment Service Office (PPESO) which handles the job placement of graduating students and job seekers, the CarDSI office will facilitate the professional and personal aspirations of students for internship in the Philippines, career p lanning and decision -making, and admission to the graduate school.
