chunks.content: Provide persons with entrepreneurship qualities access to the various livelihood and self -employ ment program offered by both government and non -government organization.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Refer jobseekers for skills training to various training center if he/she is willing to undergo the said training. e.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Make follow -ups to the employers regarding the status of the refer red jobseeker.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Employment Guidance and Counseling This is a process of assisting people intellectually and psychologically to make decisions about their career and act to realize it.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Coordinate with Guidance Center and Caree r Development offices in co nduct ing career guidance and employment counseling to graduating students of secondary, tertiary and technical/vocational schools. b.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Undertake employability enhancement seminars for jobseekers as well as those who would like to ch ange career or enhance their employability. c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Conduct pre -employment counseling and orientation to prospective local and overseas workers. d.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Identify occupationally set or not set jobseekers and provide appropriate referrals to them. e.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Provide employment or occupational counseling, career guidance, and mass motivation and values development activities.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Jobs Fair – is an employment strategy which brings together in one venue, the employers and jobseekers for immediate matching.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: This program is intended to help poor but deserving students pursue their education by mean of employment during su mmer and Christmas vacation. b.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Workers Hiring for Infrastructure Project (WHIP) is a program which aims to employ constituents of LGU‟s where construction project of the DPWH are located. c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Tulong at Alalay sa Taong Maykapansanan (TULAY) is a special program design for the persons with disability (PWD).
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The main objective of which is to assist them to integrate into the society by providing them access to training and employment opportunities.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Commission on Student Elections The Co mmission on Student Elections (CSE) is under the guidance and stewardship of the Office of the Student Welfare.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Commission is composed of bona fide students of Bulacan State University who are of good moral character and academic standing and are recom mended by their respective college/campus deans.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The CSE‟s main function is to facilitate and ensure fair, honest, orderly and clean Student Government Elections (SGE).
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Every year, the cond uct of SGE is facilitated by CSE and concomitant to this mandate is the preparation of official documents and budget related to the conduct of the elections.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Director of the Student Welfare (DSW) shall appoint a set of faculty advisers for the Commissi on to directly supervise their programs and activities.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The DSW and the faculty advisers of the Commission shall address all complaints and irregularities within the conduct of the elections.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Student Grievance Aside from the above -mentioned duties and functions, the Office of the Student Welfare is tasked by the OSAS to facilitate and process student grievances.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Director for Student Welfare (DSW) serves as the receiver and facilitator of complaints and grievances from students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: He/she has neithe r recommendatory nor disciplinary authority, but undertakes follow -ups on complaints and grievances referred to it.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Student Insurance Claim All students enrolled in Bulacan State University pay a student accident insurance fee as part of the miscellaneous fees.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Students are covered twenty -four hours a day, seven days a week, and anywhere in the world.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Students, or their parent/s or guardians, can claim for reimbursement of accident -related medical and other expenses covered by the insurance policy.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: It is highly recommende d that all pertinent documents should be secured and completed by the claimant in order to expedite the claim process.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 59 Procedures in Claiming Reimbursement The student or his/her representative (parent/s or guardian) must secure and accomplish a checklist form provided by the Office of the Student Welfare (OSW) for a personal accident claim.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The required documents are the following: (a) Insurance Policy (available and provided by OSW); (b) Official Receipt of Premium Payment (available and provided by OSW); (c) Endorsements/Attachments (if any); and (d) Police Report/Accident Investigation Report.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Bodily Injury Claims: - Medical Certificate; - Statement of Account/Bills issued by the Hospital; - Official Receipts of Medicines a nd Hospital Bills; and - Medical prescriptions.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Death Claims: - Death Certificate; - Funeral/Burial Receipts; and - Medical Certificate (if hospitalized) Students or his/her representative will be informed by OSW once the claim is approved and when the reimburseme nt is ready for pick -up.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: STUDENT DEVELOPMENT This office of Student Development (OSD) serves as the working arm of the Office of the Student Affairs and Services (OSAS) in implementing efficient and effective services on student organizations, arts and culture, sports development and publications and information through the different units provided by the university.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The functions of the director for student development include planning, organizing, monitoring and evaluating the performance of units in collaboration with the student development unit heads.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To create an atmosphere that will help students to develop their leadership skills and social responsibility. b.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To provide opportunities that will allow students to express and explore their potentials toward cultural promotion and art appreciation. c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To develop programs and activities that will cater to physical fitness and wellness of students. d.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Assist the Dean Office of the Student Affairs and Se rvices (OSAS) in the planning, preparation and implementation of student development programs and activities; 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Design programs that provide quality basic servic es that will ensure the development of students; 3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Direct, monitor, and evaluate regularly the services and programs of the Office of Student Org anizations and Activities, Office of the Student Publications and Information and Cultural and Arts Affairs an d Office; 4, Imple ment policies on student development in collabo ration with the its unit heads for better up -keeping of student development program; 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Attend to the daily concerns and issues of the different heads of the student development units; 6.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Coordinate to all academic heads the other needs of students which are relevant to student development; 7.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Oversee the integration of various programs of student development units that will enrich t he academic and community life of all students; 9.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Report directly to the dean of SAS all concerns that requires immediate action; 10.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Hold co -chairmanship to various committees that are related to student development, and 11.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Perform other related functio ns as may be directed by the Office of the Student Affairs and Services.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Offers programs for the development of students‟ potentials in areas involving organizations and activities, publications and information, cultural affairs and p erforming arts, and sports, and recognizing students‟ contribution to the Universit y (in the aforementioned areas); 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Verifies and recommends for approval of co-curricular and extra -curricular activities of the students within and off -campus; 3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Examines essential documents in sending students outside the University fo r their Official Representation; 4.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Provides assistance to the OSAS in terms of sending students (leaders, athletes, artists or journalists) in the international exchange programs and competi tions to better promote the university in partic ular and the country in general; 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Facilitates various invitations from different organizations and awards giving bodies, such as; Ten Outstanding Students of the Philippines (TOSP); Ayala Young Leaders Cong ress (AYLC); Gintong Kabataan Awards (GKA) and other student -recognition bodies; 6.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Assists the Dean of SAS reviewing the Student Handbook regularly and recommend revision as the neede d.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 61 Operating Procedures: Living up to its name, the Office Student Development makes it a duty to develop the students by which the U niversity they belong to.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: As such, the office has implemented a strict policy to students and/or group of students, who will be participating outside the campus premises.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: These activities are not just limited to contests; rather, it also includes trainings, seminars, workshops, and invited guesting.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Although the selection of the participants for a contest is dependent on the office and/or organization they are members to, it is the Office Student Development ‟s guidelines students will have to follow: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Student -participants must first acquire a Compliance Checklist in order to determine the necessary requirements for the approv al of their departure.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: This document may either be provided by the officer -in-charge of the office and/or organization, or student -participants may visit the Office of Student Development to obtain a copy.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The necessary blank lines will have to be given th e accurate and complete information, from the name of the organization to the contact nu mber of the President of organization itself.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Upon completing the data, the student -participant must obtain all the documents enlisted within the Compliance Checklist, and must be attached with the checklist itself.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Deliver the checklist and all the attached documents to the Student Development Office to be checked by the director.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Upon the c ompletion of the Compliance Checklist, deliver it to the Dean of Students Affairs for a Recommending Approval.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Once done, the Compliance Checklist will have to be brought to the University President for the signature of approval.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Although the Office of St udent Development contains specific share of duties, services and responsibilities, it is still an umbrella term that describes the general development of the students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: It is for this reason, that there are sub offices existing to provide further and speci alized services catering to a specific aspect of students‟ development.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: STUDENT ORGANIZATIONS AND ACTIVITIES Bulacan State University is providing relevant formation to students in both academic and non -academic spheres of student development.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Studen t activities that are integrated within one‟s participation in student organizations foster and enhance relevant skills and talents responsive to the imminent call for nation building.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 62 The Office of the Student Organizations and activities (OSOA) envision s molding student leadership in organized groups by designing co -curricular activities and providing opportunities for individual and collective recognition.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To educate the people and inculcate in their minds the sense of nationalism and cu ltural consciousness and enthusiasm through performing arts and literary arts.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To bring a significant message that will suffice the sociological, psychological, moral and spiritual needs of man.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To use and enhance the artistic skills of students, fac ulty members, personnel and staff in the field of arts.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To instill in the hearts of the students the aesthetic values of the arts through performa nces, visuals and literary; and 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To provide workshop, seminar, training and other benefits to deserving members of political, cultural and religious organizations on the basis of their needs and qualifications.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Initiates academic and non -academic activities that will help develop the leadership sk ills and potentials of students; 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Coordinates the programs, projects and activities of the different r ecognized student organizations; 3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Plans, supervises and evaluates all co -curricular and extra -curricular undertakings of the stud ents; 4.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Helps promote closer relations among faculty members, administration officials, non -academic personnel, and students; 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Initiates activities that will lead to the formulation of policies for the general welfare and interest of students; 6.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Develops information system on student organizations, activi ties and development; 7.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Evaluates and recommends student organization for accreditation and recognition, suspension of operation or disestablishment; 8.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Advising Support The OSO A staff is available on an appointment basis to provide assistance to student organization leaders and University adviser on organizational, leadership and programming issues.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: OSO A can also assist the organization in looking for possible and available student organization advisers.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Leadership Skills Development The OSO A offers a variety of workshops and programs designed to help student organization members develop better organizational and leaders hip skills.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: OSO A has the mandate to empower students by seeking and providing 63 opportunities for holistic leadership formation such as regional, national and international fora and competitions.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Data Banking of Student Organization List The OSO A maint ains a database of all current recognized University wide and College Organizations including files and documents of their respective accomplishment and financial reports.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Although information regarding these documents can be obtained by the public, it is also important to seek first the permission of OSO A and the organization concerned through a formal written request.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: BulSU -OSO EXECUTIVE BOARD The Core group‟s function is to help the office in planning, monitoring, executing and sourcing funds in all its activities for the whole year.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Indeed, the success of the office‟s activities is attributed to these young people who share their talents and fresh ideas without reservation.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Leadership training and other capability building ac tivities for officers and members of student organizations; 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Consultancy services to student organizations for their organizational development; 3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Evaluation and distribution of awards for outstanding student organizations and leaders.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Operating Procedures: The University believes that membership to student organizations fosters valuable experiences for students that lead to significant learning and development and helps create a sense of belonging to the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: These activities and experiences complement the formal curriculum and provide wide opportunities for enhancing personal skills.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Policies on Student Organizations Recognizing th e value of student organizations in complementing the formal academic programs of the University, it implements the following policies on student organizations: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Membership to student organizations, including those affiliated with an extramural organization, should be voluntary and open to all students of the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The membership, policies, and actions of a student organization should be determined by vote of only those persons who hold bona fide membership in that organization.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: University recognition creates an official relationship with the University and is a precondition for any student organization to operate in any of its campuses.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Recognition, as a privilege granted to student organizations by the University, affords them access to University c ampus resources and facilities in coordination with the concerned personnel.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Recognition of the student organizations in no way implies that the University takes responsibility for the actions and activities of the organizations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The purpose of recognition must be consistent with the laws of the land and policies developed by the Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS) 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The organization must maintain a membership of at least eight regular enrolled students in good standing at the University and must have at least fifteen (15) members when applying for recognition.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Recognition may be granted to local chapters of national or regional organizations provided that the national or regional organization d oes not deny membership on the basis of race, religion, national origin, ethnicity, color, age, gender, marital status, citizenship, sexual orientation, or disability.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Before a student organization is granted official recognition, a faculty or member a non -teaching personnel working as a full -time must agree to serve as an adviser to the organization.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Application for Recognition Any group of at least fifteen (15) University students may form an organization and file application for recognition as a new student organization.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Existing student organizations should file an application for renewal of recognition to the Office of Student Organizations (OSO A) within the months of August and September.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: New Organization seeking for Recognition The followi ng are the requirements for the application for recognition of a new organization: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: List of interim officers, adviser/s and members, their courses and year levels, student/employee numbers, contact numbers, addresses, e -mail addresses and signatures; 4.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Written proposed activities for the entire school year including the tentative dates of implementation and a brief description of each activity; 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Letter of invitation t o faculty/personnel to serve as organization adviser signed by the organization president or his representative; 65 6.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Signed letter from the chosen adviser/s addressed to the Head for the Office of the Student Organizations (OSO) accepting his role in the organ ization; and 7.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Copies of voluntary membership form signed by the members (Organization with 50 and above members may ONLY submit a hard copy of membership list).
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Renewal of Recognition The following are requirements for the application for renewal of t he recognition of existing student organizations: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Updated profile of officers and adviser/s, their respective positions, courses and year levels, student/employee numbers, contact numbers, addresses, e -mail addresses and signatures; 4.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Written report of accomplishment of the preceding year including audited financial statement duly signed by the treasurer, auditor, president and faculty adviser/s; 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Written proposed activities for the next school year including the tentative dates of implementation and a brief description of the activity; 6.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Letter of invitation to faculty/personnel to serve as organization adviser signed by the organi zation president or his representative; 7.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Signed letter from the chosen adviser/s addressed to the Head for the Office of the Student Organizations (OSO) accepting his role in the organization; and 8.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Affiliation with Off -Campus Organization Any group of students or organization may affiliate with an extramural or off-campus organization and apply for recognition as a local chapter, provided, that the objectives and practices of such organization do not conflict with the goals, policies and regulations of the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Within two (2) weeks after the filing of application for recognition, the OSO A will notify the organization of the status of its application.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the proposed organization meets all the requirements, it shall be granted recognition.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: List of Recognized Organizations The OSO A shall post at designated bulletin boards in the University premises and c ause the publication in the University paper the updated list of student organizations granted with University recognition.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Balangayan Sa Unibersidad: Organizations’ Week The OSO A shall hold an annual program for a ceremonial mass awarding of the cer tificates of recognition for the new school year to all organizations within the month of September.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The week -long events showcase all the activities of the office and its organizations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Organization’s Constitution and By -Laws The provisions of the con stitution of any student organization shall conform to the existing rules and regulations of the University and must include, but not limited to the following: a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A provision stating that the policies, activities and finances of the organization are subject to the control of the majority of its voting membership. i.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The procedures for decision -making in the name of the organizat ion (must include the determination of a quorum); j.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Name Change Any organization wishing to change the name under which it was originally recognized should submit a letter to the OSO A requesting that the organization be re cognized under the new name.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The letter should indicate whether the constitution will be altered, and should be signed by the president and the adviser/s.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Grounds for Denial of Recognition The application of recogni tion of student organizations shall be denied on any of the following grounds: a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Non-compliance with the requirements enumerated in seeking for and/ or renewal of recognition. b.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Evidence showing non -compliance on the submission of documents required at the end of each school year by the OSO A. c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Implementing/undertaking programs projects or activities without prior approval of the University officials who are in -charge d of the management and supervision of said organization. d.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Poor or unsatisfactory performance for the past two academic years in carry ing out its programs, projects and activities in accordance with the goals and objectives of its constitution and by -laws.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 67 An appeal may be filed by the organization to the Dean of Student Affairs and Services within ten (10) days after receiving written notification from the OSO A.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The written appeal must state all reasons why the student organization disagrees the decision of the OSO A Head and/or Dean of OSAS.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Dean of OSAS may meet with organization representatives or make his/her decision based on t he information submitted by the OSO A Head.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Written notification of his/her decision and any sanctions shall be sent to the organization within ten (10) working days after having received the appeal.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any student organization, which do not seek or renew its recognition, shall not be allowed to operate and shall be subjected to the rule on disestablishment.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Loss of Recognition Any bonafide student, faculty member or official of the University may file written, sworn complaint against any student organization for violating the policies, rules and regulations of the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Such complaint must be filed with the OSO A including the supporting documents or affidavits.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If in the view of the OSO A the co mplaint has merit, it shall forward the complaint within five (5) working days from the receipt thereof to the Dean for Student Affairs and Services for evaluation.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If a formal hearing becomes necessary, the Board of Student Discipline shall conduct the fo rmal hearing and resolve the complaint in accordance with procedure set forth in this Handbook.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A student organization‟s recognition may be withdrawn or suspended if the organization is found to have violated the policies, rules and regulations of the Un iversity.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If a student organization‟s recognition is withdrawn or suspended, the OSO A shall issue a notice to that effect to inform the academic community.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any suspended student organization shall cease to operate from the date the suspension has become fi nal and executory.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: After the period of suspension, the organization may re -apply for recognition subject to the requirements and procedure for a new organization.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any organization that repeatedly violates the policies and regulations of the University sh all be banned from operating in the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Classification of Recognized Student Organizations The University aims to establish an environment where student organizations thrive in a meaningful co -existence.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: In nurturing such environment, the Unive rsity respects every student‟s right to association in the same breath that it promulgates just policies and procedures to protect this right from being violated and to maintain a peaceful habitat that preserves constructive meaningful interaction among st udents.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Fraternities Fraternities are officially recognized by the University as part of the community of student organizations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: These are organizations 68 primarily engaged in the promotion of camaraderie and friends hip with the distinction to uphold the maxims and virtues of their code of brotherhood.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The University believes that fraternities, like other student organizations play important roles in defining an individual‟s social character and in flourishing leaders hip skills among all members.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: However, by principle, the University reserves the right to exercise its regulatory provisions and its legal prerogatives in accrediting fraternities and implementing a policy of recognizing a maximum number of three fraterni ties.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Fraternities that are currently on the list of recognized organizations do not possess perpetual and indelible recognition similar to any student organization.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If a fraternity is found, by due process, to have committed an infraction against the Univ ersity rules and regulations provided by the Student Handbook or if they have violated the provisions stated in Republic Act 8094 also known as the “Anti -Hazing Law”, the fraternity will automatically merit disqualification from the roll of recognized orga nizations and consequently will face administrative and legal action.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Socio -Civic Organizations Socio -Civic Organizations are student groups that actively engage in promoting the civic and social interests of their members.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: They operate primarily t o provide service and mutual benefit to their community.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Socio -Civic Organizations maintain the highest standards of public service and welfare and are classified as non -profit oriented.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Political Groups These are organization s of people who share similar views about the way governance and power should be used and expressed in society.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: These organizations actively participate in the promotion of the highest good, in the pursuit of social, economic and cultural development and in the attainment of a just and compassionate society.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Lifestyle Organizations They are group of people/students brought together by a shared advocacy that affirms adequate standards of living and promotes positive expressions of individual and collective creativity.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Environmental Organizations These are organizations engaged in enforcing the protection of public health and environment through the promotion of valuable resource conservation and recovery methods.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Multi -Faith Groups These are association s/organizations that actively promote among their members the value of spiritual growth and the enrichment of faith.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: They provide activities that intend to rekindle spiritual bonds with the transcendent.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Note: All University -wide organizations are directly s upervised, monitored and evaluated by the Office of the Student Organizations and Activities (OSO A).
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: College -Based/Satellite Campus -Based Student organizations These student groups are organized under the stewardship of their respective colleges/sate llite campuses.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Integral to their existence is the responsibility to conduct programs that are responsive and relevant to the particular courses offered by the college/campus in which they belong.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: College -based/satellite campus -based student organizations play key roles in extending the academic horizons of their specific fields of study by seeking opportunities of learning beyond the classroom approach through planning and implementing trainings and/or workshops that are designed to revitalize scholastic p erformances.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Note: The College/Campus Dean, Adviser and OSO A share supervision and in implementing monitoring and evaluating schemes of these organizations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Privilege of Recognized Student Organizations Student organizations recognized by the Univers ity have the privilege to: 1. use of the name of the University; 2. use University facilities, services and resources for their activities according to its established policies of university; 3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: request assistance and advisement from OSO A; 4. participate in University -sponsored programs designed to help student organization members develop better organizational and leadership skills; 5. promote the organization and campaign for membership in all campuses; 6. collect membership fees and dues and conduct fund raising projects; 7.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: develop entrepreneurial skills to sustain the economic status of the organization; and 8. sponsor student convocations and programs and invite outside speakers.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Responsibilities of Recognized Student Organizations All student organizations granted reco gnition by the University has the responsibility to: 1. accept and observe University‟s rules, regulations and policies; 70 2. operate according to the requirements of their own constitution and by laws; 3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: utilize dues and other membership fees for the benefit of the ir members and the attainment of their goals; 4. ensure the safety and general welfare of all members and guests in all their activities; 5. actively pursue programs and activities in keeping with the social, cultural, recreational and educational mission of the University; 6.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: apply for renewal of recognition and subm it yearly accomplishment report, and 7. cooperate with OSO A in the implementation of University programs for student organizations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Multi -Faith Services The university upholds the constitutional provi sion on free exercise of religion (Article II, Section 5 of Philippine Constitution).
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: In consonance, the OSOA does not discriminate nor prescribed particular religious practices or affiliation to be observed in the university.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Hence, the university encoura ges and recognizes the freedom of students to establish organization of religious nature, subject to the compliance set forth by the Office of Student Organization and Activities.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Recognized Faith -based organizations are expected to provide programs and ac tivities to enhance spiritual growth and encouraged to conduct inter -faith activities to promote camaraderie and mutual respect.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Each organization is encouraged to conduct econo mic mobility program to promote their advocacies and ensure the welfare of the members.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Concomitant to the recognition awarded to student organizations, is the privilege of the organization to design, propose and implement their economic mobility program t o further finance the plans they have proposed during the renewal of their recognition.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Costing and selling of merchandise (organization shirts, lanyards, ID holders and the like) must be considerably reasonable price.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Approval of Activities All activ ities of recognized student o rganizations whether on -campus or off-campus are considered school -sanctioned.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: These activities must ensure the preservation of virtues and integrity upheld by the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All leaders and members of recognized student organ izations must abide by the policies and guidelines of the University Student Handbook.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The student organization leader (president, chairman) and adviser must sign the written request as proof of their knowledge, consent and endorsement of their proposed ac tivity.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: In the absence of the leader and/or adviser, an officer -incharge duly appointed may sign on their behalf.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: In this regard, an explanatory 71 letter must be attached stating the reason and condition of the unavailability of the leader and/or adviser.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Once approved, the organization may proceed with the next step to hold the activity.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If pending or disapproved, the organization must clarify, act and comply on the comments, remarks, suggestions and additional requirements specified by the returned documen t.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Once all these concerns are properly addressed, the organization would be able to secure approval.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Other aspects of the activity such as venue, logistics, promotional materials, marketing are subject to the approval of the supervising office or agency.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Use of University facilities is a privilege and not a right provided to recognized student organizations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: It is highly recommended that reservation of these facilities must be secured ahead of time and must be coursed through their respective supervising o ffice.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Student organizations must follow the respective guidelines set forth by the supervising office in availing and using the services facilities.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any recognized stud ent organization wishing to sponsor an event on campus that is open to the campus community or to the general public should submit a written request a ddressed to the Vice President for Academic Affairs, signed by the organization president and adviser, not ed by the Head of OSO A and the Director for Student Development and recommended by the Dean of the SAS.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: In general, an event will be approved if it complies with the following conditions: a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The unit head responsible for the facilities requested has provided written permission for their use; d.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The sponsoring organization agrees to all special physical arrangements,; security requirements, time restri ctions, procedures and safeguards deemed necessary by the University, and financial obligations; and e.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The organization sponsoring the event shall be held responsible for complying with the conditio ns set for the event.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Failure to do so may result in a number of sanctions including, but not necessarily limited to, the loss of the right to sponsor similar events in the future, loss of recognition or financial payment for damages to property.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Student organizations choosing to sponsor an event off -campus should submit a written request addressed to the University President, signed by the organization president and 72 adviser, endorsed by the college/campus dean, noted by the OSO A Head and Director for Student Development, and recommended by the Dean of SAS and the Vice -President for Academic Affairs.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Organizations utilizing off -campus facilities are solely responsible for all contractual agreements they enter into.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any recognized s tudent organization that undertakes the sponsorship of an event should fulfil the Compliance Checklist and accepts responsibility for maintaining proper conduct of those in attendance.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Officers of the organization are responsible for informing members of the organization of this requirement.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any untoward event that can impair the name of the University or other student organizations may be a ground for disciplinary action by the University against the organizations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Bulacan State University is committed to free discussion of ideas and issues in open forum in conformance with its policies.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Student organizations may sponsor visiting speakers at their own expense provided, that outside speakers invited to the campus will contribute to educational and citizenship values and the pursuit of truth, and not to any propagandizing purpose and recruitment to underground movement that may cause harm and danger to the studentry and the University as a whole.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The use of University facilities by student organizations is a privilege and not a right.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Only recognized organizations shall be allowed to use the University facilities.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The regularly scheduled instructional class program of the University must have first priority in the use of its facilities.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Request to use University facilities should be filed at least one week in advance.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Before the OSO A approves the activity to be held on campus, the student organizations must first communicate to acade mic/unit head in -charge of the facility needed for the activity to ensure its availability.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: University facilities requisitioned in the name of a recognized student organization may only be used by that organization.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Rooms cannot be reserved in the name of one organization for use by another organization.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any organization that reserves or uses a room for any unauthorized purpose may lose its reservation privilege.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Recognized student organizations using a campus facility are responsible for its proper care.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Fund Raising Activities of Recognized Student Organizations To secure official permit to conduct fund raising activity, the sponsoring organization must file a formal letter of request to the University President, signed by the organization president and adviser, endorsed by the college/campus dean, noted by the OSOA Head and Director for Student 73 Development, and recommended by the Dean of SAS and the Vice -President for Academic Affairs.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) permit, in case the fund raising activity shall also be done outside any University campus.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A student organization is allowed to have only one (1) major fund raising activity in the academic year.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: An organization that has been granted permit to solicit fund must file a financial report with the OSO A two (2) weeks after its conduct.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Standard Policies and Procedures on Writing Communicatio n Letters for all Student Organizations 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All Presidents and Leaders of recognized clubs and organizations must be the principal authors and signatories of their respective letters.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Advisers of recognized clubs and organizations hold the duty and respon sibility of noting and signing these letters.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: They are reminded, however, to check the content of these letters before signing and to ensure that these letters conform to the standards and guidelines prescribed by the Bulacan State University Student Handb ook.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: It is also recommended that advisers must take the initiative of ensuring quality to all communication letters by checking their form and style, grammatical composition included and the like..
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: In case that a student organization has two or three adv isers, only one adviser is required to note and sign the letter.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: It is the responsibility, however, of the student organization to inform all their advisers and enter on an agreement on who will be the official signatory in every communication letter.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: These letters must be furnished in triplicate copies, each letter must be attached with a furnished activity proposal form (first copy belongs to the recipient, second copy to be kept for file by the student organi zation and third copy will be submitted to the BulSU -OSO A for filing and records purpose keeping).
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the purpose of the letter is to pursue an on -campus event and activity, the letter should be addressed to the VPAA, signed by the following persons -in-authority: a) Stude nt Organization President/Chairman as the author b) Noted by the Adviser c) Noted by the OSO A Head and Director for Student Development 74 d) The Dean of OSAS for his Recommending Approval.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the purpose of the lette r is to pursue an extramural or an off campus event and activity, the letter should be addressed to the University President signed by the following persons -in-authority: a) Stude nt Organization President/Chairman as the author b) Noted by the Adviser, OSO A Head, Director for Student Development, c) The Dean of OSAS and the Vice President for Academic Affairs as signatories for Recommending Approval d) The University President as the final signatory endowing her permission and Approval.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the purpose of the letter is to pursue an on -campus event and activity, the letter should be addressed to the VPAA signed by the following persons -in-authority: a) Stude nt Organization Preside nt/Chairman as the author b) Noted by the Adviser c) Endorsed by the College Dean d) Noted by the OSO head and Director for Student Development e) The Dean of OSAS endowing his Recommending Approval.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the purpose of the letter is to pursue an extramural or an off campus event and activity, the letter should be addressed to the University President signed by the following persons -in-authority: a) Stude nt Organization President/Chairman as the author b) Noted by the Adviser and endorsed by the College Dean c) Noted by OSO Head and Director for Student Development d) The Dean of OSAS and the VPA A as signatories giving their recommending a pproval e) The University President as the final signato ry endowing her permission and a pproval.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the purpose of the letter is to pursue an on -campus event, affair and or activity, the student organization must seek the written approval of the Campus Dean manifested in the same letter.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: In other words, the Campus Administrator shall be th e principal signatory stamping the approval of the activity.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the purpose of the letter is to pursue an extramural or an off campus event and activity, the letter should be addressed to the VPAA signed by the following persons -in-authority: a) Student Organization President/Leader as the author b) Noted by the Adviser and Local Student Affairs Coordinator 75 c) The Campus Administrator as the signatory giving his/her approval e) Noted by the OSO Head and Director for Student Development f) The Dean of SAS as s ignatory for recommending approval g) The University President as the final signatory endowing her permission and Approval.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Note: All activities must be documented including a narrative report, itinerary and pictures of all undertakings.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Student Organi zation Adviser No student organization shall be granted University recognition without adviser/s who voluntarily accept that role in the organization.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The adviser/s must be a regular faculty/non -academic personnel of the University, preferably one who is k nowledgeable on the types of the activities of the organization.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The term of the adviser for any student organization shall be for one school year and may be renewed.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the adviser withdraws before his term is completed, the organization may choose a ne w adviser to serve the unexpired term of the former.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A student organization may have one or more (maximum of three) advisers provided, however, that in case there are two advisers, one of them shall be the senior adviser; in case that there are three adv isers, one of them shall be the chairman of the Board of Advisers.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Criteria in Selecting a Student Organization Adviser All recognized student organizations are required to have an adviser or set of advisers (maximum of three).
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: It is expected, however, that all recognized organizations ensure that the following criteria are respected and met: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The adviser has an experiential background on the organization and can be considered a resource person who is willing to share knowledge in the development of the organization.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The adviser must be a part of the BulS U community and is serving the University on a full -time status. a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: University -wide Organizations and College -based Organizations must have at least one regular faculty adviser (on permanent or temporary status of employment). b.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If a College or Satellite Campus is unable to provide a regular faculty as an adviser to any of its organizations, a part -time faculty, who at least had an experience se rving the University for two -consecutive semesters, can be a qualified adviser.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The adviser must be willing to serve the organization for a minimum of one (1) year.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the contract of a part -time faculty adviser is terminated by the University, his/her ser vices with the organization follows suit.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: He/ She, however, must settle all pending obligations 76 with the organization prior to obtaining a clearance from the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The adviser must be available for consultations and meetings with the organization.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The adviser is able to guide the organization particularly in achieving the goals and programs of the organization in a given year.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Responsibilities of the Adviser The adviser/s of an organization have the following responsibilities: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Makes himself/herse lf available for consultation to all members and officers of the organization; 2.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Assists in the planning of activities of the organization to ascertain that the activities are aligned with organizational objectives; 4.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Joins the members of the organization in their activities especially if such activities are held off -campus or when the organization goes out of the campus to represent` the University; 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Fosters unity and camaraderie among officers and members of the organizati on and serves as arbiter of all internal conflicts.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Replacing an Ineffective Adviser Occasionally, the adviser of a student organization does not meet the expectations of the organization or fails to fulfill the responsibilities of the adviser.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If a student organization feels that their adviser is ineffective, the organization officers shall bring the matters to the head of OSO A to discuss the problems or issues invol ving their adviser.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If a student organization and their adviser are still unable to resolve the issue/s after bringing the matters to the head of OSOA, the organization can vote to remove the adviser, provided it is stipulated in the student organization‟s constitut ion.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Organization’s Responsibility to the Adviser The organization has the following responsibilities to their adviser: a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To keep the adviser informed concerning the overall program of the organization; b.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To notify the adviser well in advance of the sc hedule of meetings and events; c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To give the adviser an opportunity to express an opinion on issues which affect the welfare of the organization and the prestige of the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Publicity Regulations All student organizations must observe the followi ng specific regulations in their promotional activities: 77 a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: No organization poster, banner, billboard, flyer, newsletter or any printed matter may be displayed or circulated in the University campuses and premises without the OSO A approval. b.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: No signs, regard less of the type of paint or chalk, may be painted on the sidewalks or on the walls of the University buildings.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Bulletin boards on campus shall not be used to publicize off -campus politica l candidates or issues. d.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: It is the responsibility of the concerned organization to remove their posters, banners, billboards and similar materials the day after last day of the period they were allowed to be displayed by the OSO A.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Any organization that violates the above regulations shall be subject to disciplinary action as mandated and sanctioned by Bulacan State University Student Manual.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All student organizations must comply with the rules and regulations set by the OSOA regarding the posting of flyers, brochures, announcements and other information advertisements.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A letter of intent to post addressed to the Head of the Office of the Student Organizations and Activities must be prepared and signed duly by the proper persons in authority within the organization (e.g.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The letter of intent must be subm itted together with the original copy of the posting material to the OSO A for approval and clearance.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Once the approval and clearance are secured, the posting materials may be disseminated or posted on specific and certain posting ar eas within the premises of the U niversity.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: However, posting on college buildings is within the jurisdiction of the dean concerned.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Therefore, permission from the college dean must be requested prior to the posting.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: It is within the right of the dean to specify designated areas f or posting in the building and students must comply with this.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Adhesive materials to be used in the posting are only limited to scotch tape and masking tapes and push pins for cork boards to avoid tainting of university facilities particularly building wal ls.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: A posting material is only granted a duration of two (2) weeks and must not exceed.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The request, however, can be renewed if seen that the posting needs a longer duration.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Failure to comply with these set rules will be given appropriate action a s mand ated and sanctioned by Bulacan S tate University Student Manual.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Accomplishment and Financial Reports At the end of the school year, all recognized student organizations must submit their respective accomplishment and financial reports to OSO A for clearance and renewal.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Accomplishment reports must contain the documentation of all activities and programs initiated within the particular school year.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The documentation must include approved letters, activity proposals, photos, certificates and a short write up on each activity conducted.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If necessary, the OSO A Head will ask a representative of the organization to personally discuss their accomplishment report.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The f inancial report must manifest the sources and uses of the organization‟s funds with correspond ing support documents and receipts.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: This report must put into detail the cash receipts and cash disbursements per project and fund balance of the student organization at the end of the school year.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Finance Officer or the Treasurer of the student organi zation must prepare the financial report.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: This report must be reviewed carefully, signed and noted respectively by the president and adviser of the organization.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Triplicate copies, including the original copy, of the financial report must be furnished and submitted to OSO A secretariat for review.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: OSO A secretariat will check whether all financial transactions are appropriately supported by pertinent documents.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Once everything in the report is found to be in order, OSO A secretary forwards the report to the OS OA Head and the Dean of OSAS for approval.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Accomplishment and Financial Reports must be submitted complete and in order, if found otherwise, they will be returned to the concerned organization for resubmission.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Once approved, the student organization is cleared of its accountabilities and is entitled for renewal.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Statement of Account/ Billing Statement and Delivery Receipt with Official Receipts 5.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Reimbursement Expense Receipt (for exp enses incurred not higher than 1,000 pesos) 6.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Parking and toll fee tickets Note: Receipts printed on thermal paper must be photocopied immediately upon concl uded transaction.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Non -Recognized Student Organizations These refer to any organization composed of bona fide BulSU students that are not recognized due to the following: a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Lack of substantial merit on the reason and purpose o f forming the organization; and c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Organizations that fail to renew their accredited status due to non compliance of submitting their year -end accomplishment and financial reports.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Non-Recognized organizations are not allowed to carry the University name in any of their activities and/or dealings with groups, offices, and agencies inside or outside the University.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: They are not allowed to pursue and conduct activities that are similarly being undertaken by and are patented to recognized student organizations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: As bona -fide students of the University, they may also gain access to University facilities provided that they have complied with the policies of the supervising/affiliate office responsible in the maintenance thereof.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All Non -Recognized groups and organi zations must seek permission from the Dean of Student Affairs and Services before the conduct of their activities.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Student Assemblies The University upholds the student‟s right to peaceably assemble and petition the lawful authorities for redress of th eir grievances and exercise freedom of speech and expression.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The exe rcise of such rights however, should be within the bounds of law, public policy and accepted customs and traditions and should not infringe upon the exercise of others‟ rights to engage i n their studies, work or other lawful a ctivities.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Requirements for Holding St udent Assemblies Student assemblies may be held within the University campuses and its premises provided, however, that the holding of such assemblies adhere to the following cond itions: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Written request for permission to hold student assembly must be submitted to the University President at least two (2) school days before it is held or a nnounced.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If the purpose of the assembly is for redress of grievances against University authorities or policies, students shall resort to other peaceful means like dialogue or negotiation b etween parties prior to the holding of a st udent assembly.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To initiate a formal negotiation, students may 80 submit to the University officials a position paper indicating therein the grievance or issues to be discussed and proposals for the resol ution.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: In holding student assembly for the redress of grievances, the student participants must adopt the fo llowing measures: a.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To identify, in c oordination with the Dean for Student Affairs and Services, the appropriate place for the asse mbly; b.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To police their ranks and ensure that only the University studen ts participate in the asse mbly; c.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To ensure order in assembly and that no person in the student assembly molests, intimidates, or threatens other members of the academic comm unity; d.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To ensure that the student assembly does not block the gates or the roads w ithin the ca mpus; e.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To ensure that the regular academic programs and activities in the University are not in any way disturbed, distracted, or interrupted; f.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To see to it that no student under the influence of liquor or prohibited drugs join and partic ipate in the assembly; g.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To see to it that no student carries deadly weapon i.e. guns, knives etc. in the assembly; h.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: To see to it that the student assembly stays in the place and disperses on the date and time specified in the written notific ation.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The sponsoring g roup or organization shall be accountable for the restitution/restoration of any loss/damage to University facilities resulting from the holding of the asse mbly.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: This is without prejudice to whatever appropriate legal action the University may pursue again st the person(s) responsible for such loss or destruction.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 82 Student Publications and Information Policies: Congruent with the Camp us Journalism Act of 1991 (RA 7079), the Office of the Student Publications and Information implements the following policies: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Pacesetter shall be the official university student publication, which shall be financed from the school paper fee paid by th e students.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The student publication fees collected shall be treated as special trust fund and shall be used exclusively for that purpose subject to the usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The manner of disbursing student publication funds is subject to the approval of the U niversity administration.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The selec tion of editorial staff of the U niversity publication must be in conformity with the provisions of the Campus Journalism Act (RA 7079).
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The official university publication does not precl ude the production of other student publications at the different colleges and the external campuses.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Sourcing of funds for the student publications at the different colleges and external campuses shall be with the authorization of the U niversity administr ation.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The disbursement of the said funds is also subject to the accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All student publications must submit an annual budget proposal addressed to the University President, signed by the publication‟s editor -in-chief and adviser, endorsed by the college/campus dean, noted by the Head of Student Publications and Information and Director for Student Development, and recommended by the Dean of SAS and the Vice -President for Academic Affairs.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The student publications sha ll define their vision, mission, and goals for the information and guidance of all the members of BulSU academic community and the public.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Such vision, mission, and goals should direct to support the U niversity in the improvement of instruction, promotion of research, and involvement in meaningful activities of the community.

chunks.content: 83 Selection of Staff Members The selection of the editorial board members of the student publication shall be done by an ad hoc committee.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The selection committee shall be composed of the Head for Student Publications and Information, one faculty member, one media practitioner, who is acceptable to both school administration and outgoing editorial board, and two past editors to be chosen by the outgoing editorial board.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The selection committee s hall prepare and administer examinations and conduct interviews of all applicants.

chunks.content: The list of names of those who qualify shall be submitted to the Committee on Student Publications and Information, which shall endorse them for designation by the President.

chunks.content: Qualifications of Editorial Board Members To qualify as applicant to any position in the editorial board (for both the university publications and college papers), the students must meet the following qualifications: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Must have enrolled at least 15 units of academic load as certified by the unit head concerned or the University R egistrar.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The term of office of the members of the editorial board and publication staff shall be one year, unless sooner removed for cause after due process.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The members of the succee ding editorial board must be selected not later than 30 days prior to the end of the current academic year.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: In the event of vacancy in any of the positions in the editorial board due to illness, transfer, removal from office, resignation, or other simila r causes, the same shall be filled immediately.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The Head for Student Publications and Information may recommend replacements for such position to the Dean for Student Affairs and Services for his approval.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The functions, duties, responsibilities, and priv ileges of the members of editorial board shall be subject to policies and rules implemented by the administration.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Removal from Membership to the Editorial Board The members of the editorial board and staff are expected to effectively perform their roles and functions as student journalists guided by truth, objectivity, and fairness.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Misrepresentation or a ffiliation of the paper with any organization outside the University without approval by the University Administration; 3.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: CULTURAL AND ARTS AFFAIRS The Cultural and Arts Affairs Office in partnership with the U niversity administration is designed to harness skills and special t alents of students in the field of music, dance, acting and other cultural and literary arts expression.

chunks.content: Operational Procedures Each rec ognized cultural groups in the U niversity follows strict implementations of their respective guidelines and by -laws in terms of the following considerations.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Selection of members ar e based on potentials and skills as screened via auditions by the coaches and trainers.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Strict attendance of members with regards to rehearsals, clinics, workshops and engagements for proper exposure of members that is expedient in the development of their talents.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All qualified members of the cultural groups availing full scholarship grant should maintain a no failing grades on their subjects.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: If one failing mark has been received by a member, he automatically receives a warning and must complete and passed the subject in the next semester.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Having three ( 3) failing marks, the members is automatically removed fr om the list of scholarship grants.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: All cultural groups represent the U niversity within and outside the campus in the local, regional and national competitions and engagements.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Each recognized group of the cultural affairs, designs programs that will enhanc e the skills and performances of every members in the light of rehearsal schedules, engagements, clinics and workshops as needed in their respective groups.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Cultural groups of respectiv e colleges and campuses of the U niversity are officially recognized and accredited by the Cultural and Arts Affairs Office provided that they have met the required standard and submitted pertinent documents.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: Members of the Cultural groups who had represented the University in the r egional and national competition and won its respective events is granted with monetary incentives as approved by the University administration.

chunks.content: STUDENT GOVERNMENT The Bulacan State University Student Government (BSU -SG) pr ovides a means for student participation in student governa nce in the University campuses.

chunks.content: It assists in the protection of the rights and interests of the indivi dual student and the student body and serves as an official voice through which students may express their opinions on issues a ffecting their rights, welf are and development.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: 87 The Student Government operates and functions according to its Constit ution and By -laws, Resolutions and Ordinances.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The BulSU Student Government consists of two (2) chambers: The Supreme Student Council which comprise of the President, Vice President (who are elected at large), Senators, and Ex -Officio Senators; and The Local Student Council which comprise of College Governors, Vice Governors and Board Members.
chunks.title: BulSU Student handbook

chunks.content: The President of the Student Government shall automatically become the student representative in the University Board of Regents.

chunks.content: LIBRARY SERVICES Bulacan State University Library offers a wide variety of useful information sources, expertise and technologies.

chunks.content: Ready Reference Questions - Considered to be one that can be answered quickly by consulting only one or two reference tools.

chunks.content: Bibliographic Verification - Similar to ready reference, except that it provides facts about publications rather than events, people, places, or organizat ion.
