chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Leave Benefits Leave of absences is defined as a right granted to officials and employees not to report for work with or without pay as may be provided by law and as the rules prescribed in Rule XVI of Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO 292 a.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Teacher’s Leave A faculty member who does not normally perform admi nistrative functions is entitled to a teacher’s leave of proportional vacation pay c onsisting of vacation and sick leaves neither of which is cumulative. b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Cumulative Leave Credits A faculty member on vacation and sick leave credit s (VSL) who regularly performs administrative functions shall be granted cumulativ e leave.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: These functions refer to those duties that are necessary in the management of the University or any of its unit. c.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Maternity Leave Maternity leave with pay shall be granted to a lega lly married female faculty member in addition to other leaves to which she may be ent itled.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Paternity Leave A legally married male faculty member may avail of seven working days paternity leave with pay within 60 calendar days from the date his legal wife goes on maternity leave (R.A.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Parental Leave Faculty member who is a solo parent is entitled for a parental leave of seven days with pay to enable him/her to perform parental duties an d responsibilities where physical presence is required as provided under Civil Servic e Commission M.C No.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Emergency Leave A faculty member on vacation and sick leave credits m ay be granted leave from duty on account of an extreme emergency situation.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Facul ty members may avail of this leave with pay for a maximum of two working days annually on account of the following: a.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Birthday Leave A faculty member may take a one day birthday leave with pay on his/her birthday or on any other working day within seven days before or a fter his/her birthday. h.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Anniversary Leave A faculty member can avail of a one day leave with pay on the anniversary of his/her first day of service in the University or on any wo rking day within seven days before or after the anniversary date. i.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Leave without Pay A faculty member may go on leave without pay, prov ided however that s/he has accumulated leave to his/her credit.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: This leave may be granted for good reason and cause and the application for leave should be filed in ad vance so that the leave will not interfere with work in the University. j.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Sabbatical Leave A faculty member from the rank of Associate Profes sor and above may avail of the sabbatical leave for a period not exceeding one yea r with full salary.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: This leave is granted to faculty members to provide them time for scholar ship pursuits and encourage conduct of research and creative works. k.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Study Leave A faculty member who desires to go on a study is g ranted study leave without pay subject to the approval of the President.

chunks.content: Faculty attendance to trainings, conferences and completion of higher relevant academic degrees along their line of speci alization coupled with provisions of incentives and rewards translate to quality educati on and high students’ academic performance.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The University implements an integrat ive development program to ensure that faculty members attain the qualifications and compe tencies they need to perform their vital role in delivering excellent instruction, conductin g multifarious, relevant functional researches, and delivery of significant extension a nd community services.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Study Grant The University supports faculty members who intend to earn a graduate degree while they continue to teach with the University through study grant subject to the following guidelines: 1.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: To minimize inbreeding and raise the quality o f faculty members with MS and PhD degrees. b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: To increase the number of faculty members with graduate degrees from top-rate Philippine universities. c.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: To increase the percentage of MS and PhD degre e graduates to 100% and 30% respectively in 5 years.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: N on-regular faculty can be eligible for study grant if s/he satisfies the qualificatio ns for temporary status. b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Must have earned a teaching performance of very satisfactory rating and at least with one year of teaching in the University for a s emester of at least 18 units. c.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Monthly allowance of P8, 000 and P10, 000 pro-ra ted for MS and PhD respectively c.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Shall enroll in a graduate degree program identi fied as a priority area and vertically aligned to the educational preparation o f the applicant. b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Qualifications and potential of the faculty memb er should be consistent with the development goals and thrusts of the University. c.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Be offered by a reputable academic institution, a Center of Excellence or Center of Development institutions which is spec ific to the program being applied for, and by SUCs at least of Level 4 ranking.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Should not carry a teaching overload (regular or special class) any day of the week f.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Should have no engagement in other activities li ke part-time teaching in other institutions and research project collaboration.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: No n-compliance would mean termination of the contract and reimbursement of to tal amount granted to him/her g.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Should retain teaching loads of 3 units a semest er within the duration of the grant. h.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The study grant shall cover a period based on th e approved program of the grantee but not exceeding 3 years for MS and 5 year s for PhD. j.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Grantees who withdraw, drop subjects or incur fa iling grades will be made to pay for the re-enrolment of said subjects k.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The study grant shall be temporarily suspended i f the faculty member incurs two incomplete grades at any given time.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: It will on ly be reinstated after the grantee earns the completion of grades for the inco mplete courses l.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The contract will be terminated if the grantee i ncurs again incomplete grades m.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Shifting to another degree program, enrollment i n two graduate programs simultaneously and or enrollment in second MS or Ph D are not allowed. n.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Not covered by the study grant are additional co urses enrolled that are not part of the curriculum or plan of study, refresher courses taken when the faculty has exceeded the duration of the program, and enrol lment in audit subjects.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Resignation or dismissal for cause from employme nt prior to the completion of the degree shall oblige the faculty to pay back all the total amount granted to him/her. p.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Upon completion of the degree, faculty member sh all serve the University for one year for every year of scholarship and any frac tion thereof or if s/he is unable to render the required service obligation, t he total amount granted to him/her shall be returned to the University. q.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The study grant shall be covered by a contract b etween the faculty member and the University.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The contract must be signed pri or to enrolment in the graduate degree program r.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: A grantee can avail of a thesis and or dissertat ion writing financial assistance of Php 15,000 and Php 35,000 after the approval of his/her research proposal. s.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: A grantee is entitled to thesis/dissertation wri ting financial assistance and or thesis/dissertation grant provided that the researc h is in line with the research agenda of the University and that the financial ass istance/grant is subject to liquidation and break-down of expenses. u.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: In case the faculty did not finish the study on time due to delayed submission of thesis/dissertation for final examination, the g rantee may seek extension of the study grant to be approved by the President v.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: A supplemental contract may be executed if the g rantee suffers from prolonged sickness subject to approval by the Presi dent 5.

chunks.content: The faculty member initiates the process by applyi ng first to the delivering institution.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Once accepted s/he may write a formal application addressed to the President thru the Director of the Office of the Fa culty and Staff Development (OFSD) duly endorsed by the Dean b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The application must be submitted to the OFSD at l east two months prior to enrolment to the master’s/doctoral program. c.

chunks.content: Application should be accompanied by the following: 1. application form 2. certification from the Dean that the faculty app licant possesses the qualifications and potentials consistent with the g oals and thrusts of the college 3. certification that the chosen degree program for the study grant is a priority area 4.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The faculty member shall furnish the OFSD a copy o f his/her grades for courses enrolled in the preceding term prior to app roval of the study grant for the current term 6.

chunks.content: Enroll in the program of study and in the field of specialization approved by the OFSD 2.

chunks.content: Maintain an academic record acceptable to the st andards of the delivering institution 4.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Follow the regular schedule of academic course o fferings prescribed by the delivering institution until s/he finishes within t he allotted time 5.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Reimburse the University the total assistance re leased to the grantee, in case of willful abandonment of the scholarship, gross mi sconduct and noncompliance with the terms and conditions stipulated in the contract 6.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Seek the University’s permission before leaving the country while the scholarship contract is still in force.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Failure to comply would mean termination of the contract and immediate reimbursement of the tuition and other fees granted to him/her.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Sabbatical Leave The University provides another avenue of professio nal renewal and development of faculty though sabbatical leave.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: It is a vital facu lty development program that provides regular faculty the opportunity for self-improvement throug h a leave of absence with full compensation following a designated number of years of consecutive service.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: A faculty is entitled to a sabbatical leave if s/he intends to c onduct research, extension, creative or scholarly works, and or engage in rest and recreati on (R and R) subject to the applicable policies of the University.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: It engages the faculty for renewal and rejuvenation, r eflection and cultivation of fresh perspectives in his/her duties as teachers.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: It prov ides the faculty the opportunity to build new professional relationship with scholars and make hi m/her current in his/her field that enhances his/her teaching.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: It provides also the fac ulty the valuable, undistracted time for research and creative works.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Through sabbatical lea ve, the University increases faculty morale, efficiency and productivity and nurture the intellectual climate of the University.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The faculty on sabbatical research, creative, and schol arly works, or extension enhances the University’s academic reputation.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Faculty member who applies for sabbatical leave for rest and recreation must be 20 years in service and at least Associate Professor a nd can be re-applied every five years.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Has earned a performance evaluation of at least a Very Satisfactory rating for the last 3 consecutive years immediately preceding the Sabbati cal Leave 2.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Applicants who are applying for sabbatical leave for the first time and or whose projects are not funded from other sources sh all be given priority. b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The applicant must be nominated by the Dean and to the OFSD for screening and for approval of the President. c.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Nomination for research, extension or creative w orks must be accompanied by proposed program of work and its details therein, the activities that will be undertaken within the specific time frame, and wher e the leave shall be spent. d.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Upon completion of the leave, the grantee must m ake a paper to be presented to the academic community related to his research. e.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The grantee must submit two weeks upon return a formal written report of his/her output and a copy of the paper he presented, copies which must be furnished the following: 1.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The grantee must submit plan of work utilizing k nowledge, experience gained from the sabbatical leave to the President and OFSD. g.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: In the event the grantee fails to complete his/h er research work, the provision on refund shall apply h.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The grant is covered by a Memorandum of Agreemen t (MOA) that shall stipulate, among others, that: 1.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The grantee shall submit to the President throug h the OFSD within one month upon his/her return a complete and detail ed report of his/her Sabbatical Leave accomplishments.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Likewise, he/she shall be required to disseminate the result of his/her study through publication and/or public presentation 2.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: In the event the grantee fails to comply with the terms and conditions stipulated in the MOA, s/he shall refund the Univer sity the full compensation received for his/her Sabbatical Leave 51 i.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The grantee shall acknowledge the University a s the source of the grant should the result of the project be published, pu blicly presented or exhibited.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Application for sabbatical leave for rest and re creation must merit justification of the purpose of the leave. k.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The entire period of the Sabbatical Leave shall be considered creditable service for purposes of promotion, seniority, longe vity, and retirement. l.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: A grantee shall not take any teaching load with or without honorarium or engage in any gainful employment outside the instit ution while on sabbatical leave. m.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: A total of three grants shall be available annua lly where applications shall be prioritized.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: A sabbatical leave may be granted for a period o f one year with full pay effective upon approval by the University.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Attendance in Conferences, Seminars, an d Training Conferences, seminars and workshops are usually of short duration.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: But, especially when they take place abroad and involve the present ation of papers, they can be of immense benefit to both the faculty member and to the Unive rsity.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: They can also serve as an incentive for joining and staying within the ranks of the fac ulty, hence, provision is made for them.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Attendance of faculty members in conferences, semin ars and training give them the opportunity for exposure and eventual collaboration with collea gues and scholars in the national and international academic communities.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Such exposures and interactio n enhance qualification and competencies of faculty members.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Cognizant of the essential role of attendance to conferences and seminar in the professional development of the faculty, the Univer sity provides financial assistance to faculty attendance to conferences and seminars.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Has academic degree aligned and or relevant to t he theme of the conference or seminar c.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Has earned at least a Very Satisfactory evaluati on rating for the last semester preceding the application for attendance to confere nces, seminars or training 2.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Requirements The following documents must be submitted together with the application: a.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Invitation from the organization or agency spons oring the conference, seminar or training 52 b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: A faculty may avail of full financial support to attendance to conferences or seminars, local or international, for a maximum of three (3) times a year. b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: A faculty on study grant is exempted from attend ing conferences and seminars as they are expected to focus on their stu dy. c.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: A schedule of make-up class which shall be condu cted by the grantee, in agreement with the students, prior or immediately a fter his/her return or application for substitution in class must accompan y application for attendance to conferences or leave. d.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Certificate of Attendance, brief summary of acti vities attended, and liquidation of expenses shall be submitted to the O FSD. f.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: As much as possible attendance to local and inte rnational conferences and seminars should not be scheduled during school regi stration days, the first two weeks of classes beginning a semester, and examinat ion days. g.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Faculty members with administrative duties shoul d limit the duration of their attendance to conferences or seminars to not more t han fifteen (15) working days, consecutive or cumulative, per semester. h.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Other forms of allowances allowed by the Philipp ine Government for local and travel abroad such as pre-travel allowance, clo thing allowance, are subject to availability of funds, accounting and auditing r ules 4.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Attendance in Training Programs Training on scholarship grants made available to t he Philippine government may be applied for by the different colleges and units of the University. a.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: 29 series of 1963, only permanent emplo yees should be nominated to foreign-assisted training/scholarship grants.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: P resent conditions, however, warrant a more flexible policy that would conform with the various international commitments of the Philippine governm ent in the pursuit of its development goals.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Employees with non-permanent app ointments, therefore, may subsequently be granted a waiver from the afore mentioned Memo Circular, provided they fall under any of the following categ ories: 1.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Employees of government agencies, including stat e colleges and universities and government-owned or controlled corporations, di rectly involved in the undertaking an implementation of foreign-assisted p rojects in which the agreement entered into and between such government agencies and the foreign entity includes training/scholarship grants for project staff members under the auspices of the lending or assisting fore ign institution.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Employees engaged in highly scientific fields cr itical to national development where there are only a few educationally qualified personnel as certified by the Department of Science and Technology 3.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Employees who have been personally invited by a sponsoring entity; or employees who are proposed for nomination by agenci es which have been directly invited by a foreign entity to avail of it s training/scholarship grants.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: If a candidate/nominee had previously availed of an y foreign-assisted scholarship/training grant from any sponsoring ent ity, in no instance shall said candidate/nominee be allowed to avail of another g rant unless s/he has completed serving the required number of years wit h the nominating agency as provided for in the scholarship contract entered into and between the candidate/nominee and the nominating agency, pr ovided such grant is not a direct consequence of her/his previous grant.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The duration of the scholarship/training grant shall not exceed the period of the candidate/nominee’s temporary or contractual ap pointment, and in no case shall it go beyond one (1) year, unless the co ntractual or temporary appointment is renewed and approved by the Civil Se rvice Commission 6.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Everyone granted authority to travel abroad shall s ubmit within ten (10) days upon return a brief written report about the trip b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Other allowances such as clothing allowance, pre-travel e xpenses, and book allowances may be granted as requested by the award ee, subject to the availability of funds, accounting, and auditing rul e.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Faculty Exchange Program The University seeks domestic and foreign linkages and collaboration with other universities to send faculty members for an e xchange program.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Faculty exchange is another avenue for the development of faculty as it enhances their teaching ability and develops their expertise.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The faculty and Unive rsity derive manifold benefits from the faculty exchange program.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Faculty exchange bene fits outweigh the cost of the program as each institution on an exchange program provides salaries and fringe benefits for its own faculty members. a.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Benefits of Faculty Exchange 1. enrichment of faculty functions of research, ins truction and extension and community service through exposure and experiences in new international academic environment 2. opportunity to meet and collaborate with other f aculty from different universities 3, exposure of BulSU students from visiting faculty in the University 54 4. increased standing of the University in the facu lty exchange program with domestic and foreign universities.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Only regular faculty can avail of the faculty excha nge program from reputable host institution (CHED recognized top schools, SUC Level 4, COE).
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Interested faculty member in exchanges should apply to the Director of International Affairs Programs who will assist h im/her in securing exchange program with prospective host institution.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Appointments of faculty to be exchanged must be rec ommended by the concerned Department Head and Dean for approval of the President.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The University will maintain the academic rank, all salary and fringe benefits of the exchanged faculty and shsall not reduce his/ her credit toward promotion or other leaves unless otherwise agreed p rior to the exchange.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The University will provide stipend to cover other expenses such as accommodation.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Faculty assignments will be agreed upon by all part ies prior to approval of the exchange.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: A faculty member on exchange shall be designated as visiting faculty by the host institution and can avail of exchanges as ofte n as approval from the University is obtained.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The unequivocal terms of exchange must be worke d out by the faculty and neither the University nor host institution will be responsible for any additional expenses which may arise from oversight or misunderstanding.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The exchanges will be covered by a contract to be agreed upon by all parties at least two months before the expected arrival of the visiting faculty.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The contract shall include liability insurance, vesting faculty rank, start and end of the exchange, and faculty assignment.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Chapter VII FACULTY RESEARCH AND CREATIVE WORK 55 BulSU through the University Research Office endeav ors to conduct researches for knowledge generation that are focused in responding to the immediate needs of the community.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: These studies are envisioned for use in academic instruction, extension projects, and bases for policy making and blueprints for prod uction.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Series of research are conducted in the interest of social justice and sustainable d evelopment to attain equity among a diverse population.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The University invests in its faculty a nd staff so they could effectively do research, present their research in local, national, and inte rnational conferences, and publish their research in reputable journals for the disseminatio n of information and consilience with other research projects outside the University.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: BulSU re cognizes that in today’s twenty-first century world, the name of the game is not just knowledge-g eneration; BulSU recognizes that for its research projects to create an impact, research mus t be consciously directed towards the fulfillment of felt and assessed needs of the Unive rsity, its immediate community, and by extension, the nation (BulSU Research Manual 2018).
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Research pertains to a wide range of scientific, th eoretical, scholarly and creative activities that encompasses a way of scientific inq uiry and application of scientific perspectives in all aspects of faculty duties and responsibiliti es.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Through research a faculty promotes inquiry, creates, disseminates, and advances new fo rms of knowledge, and contribute to the knowledge base of his/her academic field.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Research results are meant to be shared in the classroom (research-based instruction), to the comm unities (research-based extension/service), and to the larger society (publ ic presentation, publication, and patented invention).
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The research undertakings of the facul ty develop his/her teaching competence, further his/her professional development, and trans late to an enhance prestige and visibility of the University.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: These research undertakings and creative works of the faculty are demonstrated through various avenues: 1. conducting sponsored research grant or commissio ned research 2. publishing books, articles, or research reports as author, co-author, editor, co-editor.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: 3. engaging in editorial publications as journal ed itor, journal referee, book reviewers 4. delivering lectures, reading conference papers, and presenting posters 5. producing artistic works (visual art, music, cin ema), performances (theatre) and exhibitions (museums, galleries).
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: 6. crafting technological innovation and developing digital softwares 7. writing instructional materials, modules, schola rly essays or book chapters and reviews The University supports the research engagement of the faculty through the implementation of the following policies and variou s incentives which aim to meet following research goals: a.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Eligibility 56 Every faculty member regular or non-regular is enco uraged to conduct research or scholarly and or creative works.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: All research activities shall be based on the Unive rsity research agenda and program thrusts by Commission on Higher Education ( CHED), Department of Science and Technology, National Economic Developme nt Authority (NEDA), CLIERDEC, and other funding agencies. b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Faculty members who apply for research grant sha ll submit capsule research proposals in six copies to the Research and Develop ment Council (RDC) for the technical review and evaluation by the University R esearch Council (URC).
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Researchers whose capsule proposals are tentatively allocated with funds will be properly informed regarding the preparation of t he proposal in completely detailed format. c.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The detailed research proposals must be submitte d to the RDC for technical review and evaluation.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: After which, the final copie s of research proposals will be endorsed to the URC for deliberation.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The Research Director informs the researchers th rough the Deans regarding the appropriate action that must be undertaken in t he implementation of the research project. e.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Upon approval of the President of the research p roject, the proponent shall enter into a contract or Memorandum of Agreement wi th the University.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Such contract shall detail the rights, benefits, and the corresponding obligations and responsibilities of both parties. f.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: For collaborative researches, a Memorandum of Ag reement (MOA) signed by the cooperating and implementing agencies is requir ed.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Stipulated in the MOA are the objectives of the research, rights, benefit s and responsibilities of both agencies. g.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Non-compliance with any of the provision in the agreement shall be ground for: 1.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: If warranted after investigation, the researche rs may be required to reimburse any honorarium that have already been gra nted. h.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Publication or use of the University funded rese arch outputs shall require University approval. i.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Any renewal, extension and termination of the re search project should be done in writing to the funding agency.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: If the resea rch is funded by the 57 University, the letter should be addressed to the P resident, through the Vice President for Research, Extension, and Training. j.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Any research project that was directly funded by the University or was provided with manpower or facilities support will m ake the University a coowner of the research project. k.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Any commercialization of research output in whic h the University is a coowner requires approval from the VPRET Office.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Members of the academic community, with approved research proposal, shall be provided with the following administrative support: 1. adequate financia l assistance 2. adequate library resources and official endo rsement to other libraries 3.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: appropriate and adequate facilities 4. adequate time for research activities. b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Approved research proposal shall receive resear ch assistance in the form of: 1.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Facilitating requests for manpower, i.e. researc h assistants/ aides and others needed for the research 2.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Facilitating requests for materials and supplies needed in the conduct of study 4.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: To motivate the faculty members and staff to con duct an independent study, there shall be a reduction of teaching and a dministrative load that is within the established policies of the Universit y: Category Equivalent Teaching Load (hours/week) 1 research project consisting of 2 12 solo authorship but related studies 2 solo authorship studies, simultaneously 12 l solo authorship study 6 This provision covers only those studies with durat ion of not less than three months.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: For those studies that are less than three months, the teaching load equivalent may be proportionately reduced.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Faculty researchers with approved University res earch grants shall be entitled to honorarium rates per study or project w ith total honoraria of all their researches not to exceed 100% of the annual b asic salary.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: 00 1, series of 2009, are as follows: Categories of Researchers Rates of Honora ria Project Leader (at least) 1-2 projects Php10,200/mo.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Researchers may claim credit, particularly for the purpose of determining academic rank, for their research outpu ts subject to the criteria of the accrediting body concerned.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Credits for purposes of academic ranking may be claimed on research project s only after they have been completed and upon certification by the R DC Director in the campus.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The University may finance the publicati on, marketing, and distribution of research findings of researchers in books, manua ls, and other formats that can be sold.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: As such, researchers are entitled to royalties in accordance with the stipulations of the MOA between the researchers and the University.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Researchers who publish their researches in nati onal and international refereed or non-refereed journals are entitled to f inancial incentives as follows: Refereed Non-Refereed National Php20, 000 Php5, 000 International Php40, 000 Php10, 000 6.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Financial incentives for researchers who will p resent their completed researches in the institutional in-house review are the following: Presenter Php3, 000 Best Presenter Php5, 000 Best Paper Php10, 000 7.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Regional winners of Research and Development papers shall receive Ph p10, 000 each (BOR Resolution No.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Paper Presentation in International and National Conference 59 Attendance to conferences for public presentation of faculty researches forms part of the development of the competencies of the faculty and the research culture of the University.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The University extends support to facul ty members who present papers or deliver presentations in prestigious international or natio nal conferences according to the following guidelines.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: To raise the standards of research productivity in the University by encouraging faculty members to submit paper for pre sentation in reputable national and international conferences. b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: To encourage faculty members to engage in high q uality research that would result in journal publications c.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: To provide opportunities for faculty to interact and collaborate with other national and international experts in different fie lds d.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Eligibility Regular faculty members who meet the following crit eria may apply for the incentives to paper presentations in international conferences. a.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Must present an original research paper (not stu dent’s thesis/ dissertation) or invited as parallel or plenary speaker in prestigio us conference. b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The paper to be presented should be on a specifi ed discipline preferably vertically aligned to teaching specialization. c.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The paper to be presented should be of a level o f quality that meets the standards of peer review and merits publication in ISI/SCOPUS journal. d.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Pre-departure expenses to include passport appli cation/renewal fee visa application fee airport terminal fee, travel tax an d insurance 3.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The number of days is based on the durat ion of the conference plus one day before the commencement of the conference 60 6.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Per diem of US$ 100 per day appropriated for foo d local transportation and communication expenses and to cover for inadequate funds for hotel expenses.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The number of days is based on the duration of the conference plus one day before and one day after the conference. b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Paper presenters in local, regional, national or international research forum shall be given a cash incentive of Php 2000, Php 40 00, P6, 000, and Php 10,000 respectively.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The approval for paper presentation shall be bas ed on the quality of the international or national conference to be attended as to its reputation as highimpact conferences, strict acceptance rate, and dis tinguished technical/scientific committee.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: An additional indicator of high quality conferences is the significant number of paper presenters from prestigious nationa l and foreign universities. b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: A regular faculty member may avail of the grant more than once a year subject to the availability of the funds and the equitable opportunities for other faculty to present papers in international conferences. c.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Regulations on disbursement, liquidation, and ov ersight shall follow relevant accounting/auditing procedures d.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The faculty member shall submit a plan for alter native class (i.e., a brief description of how the faculty member plans to mana ge the classes to be missed while on official business) for the classes that wi ll be affected by his/her absence e.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Research paper approved for presentation in a co nference or seminar recognized by CHED or reputable professional organi zations shall be given with financial assistance. f.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The faculty member shall submit post-conference report/summary of activities and liquidate financial obligations within one week after returning from the conference 5.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The faculty member initiates the process by acco mplishing the application form addressed to the President thru the Director o f the Office of Faculty and Staff Development b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Research Journal Publications Research publications will be given incentives base d on the following classifications.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Other refereed/abstracted/indexed journal Php 40,0 00.00 (non-predatory/reputable publishers) However, the following limitations shall apply. a.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: However, authors who are connected in other univers ities or agencies are not eligible to the incentives program.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Research Journal Citations Journal citations for published research articles o f researchers will be considered as significant contributors to the research accomplish ments based on the following criteria. a.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The three period commences on date of publication of the arti cle and ends on the third anniversary of its publication.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Research Competition Winners The University recognizes the achievements of resea rchers in research competitions.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: In addition to the prizes earned from the competiti on, the researchers are eligible for the following incentives schedule.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: International 1st place Php 20,000.00 2nd place 15,000.00 3rd place 10,000.00 2.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Regional 1st place Php 5,000.00 2nd place 3,000.00 3rd place 2,000.00 However, the following limitations shall apply. a.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The incentives are for those who were declared a s rightful winners in research competitions.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Research competitions that are condu cted by government agencies, research consortia, and other CHED-recognized or en dorsed research organizations will be considered as legitimate by the University. b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The incentives are for the winners of Best Paper or Best Project award categories only.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Intellectual Property The University recognizes the efforts of the resear chers to go through the screening process for patents and copyrights by the Intellect ual Property Office of the Philippines (IPOPhil).
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Submission of NDA (non-disclosure agreement) Ph p 10,000.00 for the invention for subsequent patent search and patent draft b.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Industrial design/Trademark/Copyrights Php 20, 000.00 In addition to the incentives, the University will pay for the fee necessary to process the application.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Outstanding Researcher Award Php 75,000.00 63 Criteria for the Outstanding Researcher Award a.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Contribution to national/regional development e.g. social, economic, ecological, etc.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Contribution to productivity or cost effectivene ss and/or solution to a major concern/problem 3.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Researches to be considered for evaluation must be reported to the URO and supported by verifiable documents.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Creative Work as Research The University supports research in creative fields or creative works to complement faculty research that advances knowledge leading to useful discoveries, finds solutions to societally problems, and application to the product ion of new materials, products, new processes and system.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Research in creative work revolves on people’s socio-cultural environment, th eir humanities and social construct and apply these knowledge to the betterment of people a nd society.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Research in creative work covers those that have b een created in any of the followingwriting, constructing, composing, designing, record ing, performing or curating.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: They include the substantial production of new literary work, bo ok and or textbook writing, artistic work, novel invention, and development of innovative soft ware.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: A faculty may conduct research in 64 creative work from various disciplines in architec ture (realized, constructed, fabricated or unrealized design projects) design, creative writin g, visual arts (painting, drawing, sculpture, crafts, ceramics, textiles), performing arts (dance, music, theatre, play), film, video, live performance art, interdisciplinary arts, media and electronic arts, and digital works.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Guidelines for Creative Work Grants The University provides research grants in creative work with the intent that such creative work leads to publication, exhibitions, an d performances.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The grant is intended to support the development of creative works of facult y members for applications for external funding subject to the following guidelines: 1.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The University shall publish a Call for Submissi on of Proposal for Creative Works every first week of January of every year.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The grant may be applied for creative work accor ding to the following criteria: a.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Quality and originality of the proposed creative work b. viability for publication exhibit and performanc e c. potential for external funding 3.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Request for ex tension of completion of creative project may be approved after strong justification and subm ission of work thus far completed without additional grant.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Proposal must be submitted to the URO for techni cal evaluation by a Screening Committee composed of faculty in the University and external reviewers whose specialization and expertise are in line with creative project submitt ed.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: All reviewed proposal that pass the evaluation w ill be recommended by the VPRET to the President for funding.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Proposals that are not approved for funding beca use of lack of funds will be ranked in the next cycle of call for submission of proposal for c reative works.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Proponents can withdraw their proposals for submission to other funding agencies.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Proponent whose proposal is approved or not for grant shall be properly notified by the VPR ET.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Only regular faculty with the rank of assistant professor and higher may apply for the grant.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Faculty with poor track record of completion of approved research grant and publication shall not qualify for the grant.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The grant amount to be received by the grantee must not exceed P600, 000 which shall be awarded in the form of contract indicating the p urpose, expected output, and intellectual property rights of the parties involved.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The release of the grant shall be by three tran ches in which 35 percent of the grant shall be paid upon signing the contract, 35 percent upon submission progress report after 9 months.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The remaining balance shall be released upon submis sion of proof of deliverables such as a copy of the creative output when appropriate, proof of acceptance for publication in the case of books, or proof of exhibition in the case of fil ms, artwork and other non-written output by reputable agencies.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The University shall be acknowledged as the sou rce of the grant in all publications and exhibitions and in the application for intellectual property rights.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The grantee is responsible for the use of the f unds must comply with University regulations on financial procedures and auditing rules.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The VPRET shall form a committee to conduct mon itoring and evaluation of all approved grants to ensure timely compliance and reporting of the progress of the project by the grantees.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The grantee shall submit a one-page report to t he VPRET at the termination of the grant describing the educational and public impact of the grant and efforts done to secure external funding.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The grantee shall meet his/her obligation in th e performance his/her regular functions as faculty during the grant period.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The grantee shall return the full amount if s/h e resign before the completion of the project.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: If in case the grantee is terminated for cause, s/h e shall return to the University the unused amount.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Extension of Faculty and Academic Unit The University is founded on extension as one of it s functional pillars.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: It is the conduct of programs that extends to the community, industry, private and public agencies and larger society the knowledge resources and expertise of th e University.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Extension is undertaken by an academic unit, faculty, staff, and students outs ide of a course requirements, individually or as a group, to the people and other organization s.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Extension program is another avenue of the University and vital function of faculty in achieving excellence and contribution to regional development.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Extension programs of academic unit or faculty, sta ff and students must be based on the extension agenda of the University 2.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Extension and community service must be coordinated with the University Extension Office.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Extension must be research based and must generates, validates, and applies knowledge that can enrich instruction and research 4.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Extension programs may include services utilizing e xpertise and talent related to the specialization and expertise of the academic un it and or faculty.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: It can include transfer of technology and knowledge that impact on development of communities and organizations.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Extension can be done on the nature of skill traini ng and or certificate granting activities outside instruction and research such as non-degree training, seminars, workshops, conferences, review classes, and or lect ures 6.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Each academic unit, through the College Extension C ommittee in consultation with the faculty, shall adopt and annually update i ts extension agenda /programs based on the University extension agenda.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Each academic unit shall have an extension committe e to oversee extension activities and assist the faculty in his/her extens ion activities.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Extension activities shall be considered and credit ed in the faculty and staff academic workload subject to the policy of the coll ege 67 Chapter IX AWARDS AND RECOGNITIONS The University recognizes individual and collecti ve efforts in the discharge of the faculty functions, duties and responsibilities and rewards them for exemplary performance which translate in enhanced faculty competencies an d in the realization of the University goals.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Pursuant to the provision of Rule X of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: 292 and in line with the Revise d Policies on Employee Suggestions and Incentive Awards Systems (ESIAS) provided under Civ il Service Commission Resolution No.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: 2001, the Bulacan State University through Program on Awards and Incentives for Services Excellence (PRAISE) implements a system of awards and recognition: Section 1.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The BSU-PRAISE shall be designed to encourage creat ivity, innovativeness, efficiency, integrity and productivity in the publi c service by recognizing and rewarding officials and employees, individually or in groups for their suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments a nd other personal efforts which contribute to the efficiency, economy or othe r improvement in University operations, or for other extraordinary a cts or services in the public interest.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The BSU-PRAISE shall provide both monetary and non- monetary awards and incentives to recognize, acknowledge and reward pro ductive, creative, innovative and ethical behavior of employees throug h formal and informal mode.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: For this purpose, the BSU-PRAISE shall encourage th e grant of non-monetary awards.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Monetary awards shall be granted only when the suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other pers onal efforts result in monetary savings which shall not exceed 20% of the savings generated.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: 76 Chapter X ACADEMIC COSTUME The adoption of an academic regalia or academic dr ess is inherent in an institution of higher learning such as BulSU.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: It sp eaks of the historical educational tradition of the University and it symbolically emb odies its educational philosophy.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The wearing of an academic dress lends prestige and hon or to the University academic community as it is proudly worn by University offic ials, faculty, students and guests during formal gatherings such as convocation, comme ncement exercises, inauguration of professorial chairs, and installation of a Unive rsity President.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: The alampay is the official academic dress of BulS U and its design is grounded on the history of the University and indigenous cul ture of Bulacan and the country.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Its symbolism represents rootedness of BulSU in Philipp ine educational settings while reaching out to the trends and development of highe r education in global milieu.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Mula sa tradisyunal nitong gamit bilang balabal sa likod at balikat ng mga Tagalog, ang alampay ay patuloy na nakikisabay sa makabagong pan ahon at bumabagay sa pagbabago ng mga kasuotan at panlasa sa pananamit.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Umiinog ang alampay sa kasalukuyang panahon at nagi ng simbolong pangkalinangan at kapahayagan ng pagkakakilanlang Pilipino na sa b awat pagkakataon ay mailalangkap sa anumang kasuotan.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Ang alampay ay isang bahagi ng pananamit na nakaugn ay sa kasaysayan at kalinangan ng Pilipinas.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Kaya sa alampay maibabatay ang pagtatalaga ng isang bagong kasuotang pangakademiko ng BSU.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Sinasagisag ng Alampay bilang Damit Pang-akademi ko ng BulSU ALAMPAY- ka ALAM an, tagum PAY at kahus AY an 1.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Pagsasagawa ng mga gawain at gampanin ng buong husa y na may pangunguna bilang isang pamantasan.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Mula sa tradisyunal na alampay, ang alampay ng BulS U ay nilangkapan ng mga sumasagisag mula sa libong taong tradisyun ng pagha habing bayan (habi, hablon).
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Pinaghalawan ang pamamaraan ng habing t’nalak ng mg a T’boli at ng mga Bontok at Kalinga na nagpanday ng kalikhaang katutubo, ispiri tualidad, estetika at hiraya ng kanila kanilang pangkat.

chunks.content: Lahat ng graduates ng BulSU, undergrad o graduat e program, at mga guro at mga kawani, saan man kolehiyo o pamantasan nagtapos ay may dangal na isusuot ang alamapay. e.

chunks.content: This Faculty Policy and Governance Handbook is in consonance with the various existing University policy documents and manuals that govern the overall operation, management, philosophy and direction of the Bulacan State Unive rsity such as BulSU Administrative Code.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Any part or provision of this Faculty Pol icy and Governance Handbook which found to be inconsistent with the letter and spirit of exist ing laws, issuances, rules, and regulations shall be deemed invalid and void.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: If any part or provision herein is declared invalid, the remainder of this Faculty Policy and Governance Handbook or the application of such provision shall not be affected by such declaration.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: This Faculty Policy and Governance Handbook shall take effect immediately upon approval by the Board of Regents as endorsed by the Academic Council.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Inflation shall be considered in granting pe cuniary benefits stated in particular provision of this Faculty Policy and Governance Handbook.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: Amendment or modification of any provision this Faculty Policy and Governance Handbook can be made as may be necessary upon the d eliberation and endorsement of the Academic Council to the Board of Regent for approva l.
chunks.title: Faculty Manual for BOR

chunks.content: 2023 ENHANCED BulSU’s GUIDELINES ON ADMISSION FOR SHIFTEES, TRANSFEREES, RETURNEES, AND SECOND UNDERGRADUATE DEGREE TAKERS The Bulacan State University is committed to providing relevant and quality education for all.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Thus, policies and guidelines were created to address the needs of our students/clienteles.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: The implementation of the crafted guidelines often reveal inaccuracie s and inadequacies that compels further enhancement.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: One of such is Enhanced Guidelines on Admission of Shiftees, Transferees, Returnees, and Second Undergraduate Degree Takers which was approved by the Board of Regents (BOR) during its 2nd Regular Meetin g last May 28, 2021 bearing the BOR Resolution #35, series of 2022.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: To ensure that the University’s policies and guidelines maintain their relevance, sustainability, and adaptability to the BulSU community, the Student Policy, and Program Development Off ice (SPPDO) under the Office of the Vice President for Academic Affairs (OVPAA) in consultation with Admission and Orientation Services Office (AOSO), drafted the 2023 Enhanced BulSU’s Guidelines on Admission for Shiftees, Transferees, Returnees and Second Undergraduate Degree Takers.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Definition of Terms The following terms are defined to establish clarity in nomenclature used in the guidelines Shiftee is a student of BulSU who intends to change program or curriculum (e.g., from Education to Engineering or from Engineering to Architecture).
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Transferee is a student from another recognized institution of higher learning and is officially allowed to enroll in the same or another program in the University.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Returnee is a student who was previously enrolled at the University and opted to discontinue his /her studies for one semester or longer for valid reason/s (with or without filing the Leave of Absence or LOA.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Second Undergraduate Degree Taker is one who has comple ted an undergraduate degree in the University or in an accredited higher education institution and is enrolled in a different program of the University.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: 2 | Page B.Admission Guidelines for Shiftees Shiftees should open the application link that will be posted onli ne by the Admission and Orientation Services Office (AOSO) as scheduled and choose the identified programs with available slots.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Shifting to any program is allowed only 1.1.1 in the first semester of each academ ic year 1.1.2 for the incoming second - and third -year students from the same campus 1.1.3 for incoming 3rd year students if the shiftees are coming from any of the external campuses and enrolling in the main campus or vice versa.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Shiftees from a program of one college to a program of another college or those who intend to change only their major/program in the same college/campus are required to take the college -based shiftee’s examination.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: A student intending to shift needs to follow the procedures for shifting as specified for his/her case.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: The student who intends to shift must open the application link as provided by the Admission and Orientation Services Office (AOSO) as scheduled.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: He/She may seek advice from his/her current Program Chair for guidance and to d iscuss the reasons for shifting.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: He/she must accomplish the Shifter Application Form and the request letter addressed to the receiving dean through the receiving program chai r clearly indicating the reasons why the shifting is being applied for.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Copy of Grades issued by the University Registrar’s Office Grade Requirements: ❖ For Board Programs, no grades lower than 2.0 in all taken courses/subjects and at least a GWA of 1.75. ❖ For Non -Board Programs, no grades lower than 2.5 in all taken courses/subjects and at least a GWA of 2.0.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Note: A student with NO failing mark in the General Educa tion courses and with at least two (2) failing marks in the Specialization subjects may shift to any allied non -board program if he/she is accepted by the receiving dean and his/her request to shift is approved by the Chancellor. b.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: The shifting applicant must pass the interview and the college -based examination.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: The Dean may approve or disapprove the request based on the required criteria. ❖ If approved the applican t will be advised to start the enrollment under his/her new program. ❖ If disapproved the student may either stay in his/her current program or transfer to another HEI.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: An orientation on the new program will be given by the Program Chair/Department Head for the shiftee.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Shiftees may apply for crediting of previously taken General Education (GE) courses/subjects, Physical Education (PE), National Service Training Program (NSTP) to the Office of the Registrar.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Transferees from recognized higher education institutions may apply for admission to the University provided that: 1.1.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: The grade requirements are:point average 1.2.1 For Board Programs, an overall general weighted averageof at least 1.75 and with no grades lower than 2.0 in all taken courses/subjects.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: 1.2.2 For Non -Board Programs, no grades lower than 2.5 in all taken courses/subjects and an overall general weighted average of at least 2.0.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Note: A student with failing grade/s may transfer to any non -board program if he/she is accepted by the receiving dean and his/her request to transfer is approved by the Chancellor and is able to meet the grade requirements for non -board programs.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: 1.3 Th e applicant has completed not more than fifty (50) percent of the units required for the program.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Application Procedure 2.1 The applicant must follow the procedures provided @ iadmissions.bulsu.edu.ph, accomplish the Transferee Applicat ion Form, and prepare the documents to be submitted.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Transferee Application Form with two (2) pieces of identical 2”x 2” colored ID picture with white background and a name tag 2.2.2.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Transcript of Records or Certification of Grades 2.2.3 Certificate of good moral Character from the former school 3.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Transferring to any program of the University is only allowed in the first semester of each academic year.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: The following students are disqualified from applying for transfer admission: 4.1 Those who have been permanently disqualified from any HEI.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: 4.3 Those whose admission was withdrawn due to submission of fake/ falsified entrance credentials/ documents.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: The number of semesters that the transferee has availed of free tuition will be deducted from his/her UNIFAST grant of free tuition which is the expected duration of the course plus a one -year grace period (UNIFAST, 2018) 6.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Transferees ma y apply for credits of their previously taken General Education (GE) courses/subjects, Physical Education (PE), National Service Training Program (NSTP) to the Office of the Registrar, provided that: 6.1.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Their former HEI has a higher or equal level of AACCUP accreditation status and/or ISO certification as the University.

chunks.content: The description of the courses for credits is the same for both the other HEI and Bulacan State University.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: For confirmation, the transferee must submit a copy of th e course syllabus certified by his/her former HEI for each of the courses being applied for credits.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: The transferee must take a validation examination for every subject that he/she applying for credit.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Note: The eight General Education subjects are:1) Understanding the Self 2) Contemporary World 3) Purposive Communication 4) Art Appreciation 5) Ethics 6) Readings in Philippine History 7) Mathematics in the Modern World 8) Science Technology and Society D.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Students who discontinued their studies at BulSU for not more than six (6) semesters may be re -admitted after submission of the following documents: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: When readmitted, the returning students may 1.2.1. be allowed to continue their old curriculum if they are an incoming third year or higher year level 1.2.2. be advised to continue under the existing new curriculum and apply for crediting of courses taken such as GE general education subjects Physical Education (PE), National Service Training Program (NSTP).
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: 1.2.3. be given the most expedient way for the students to finish their program as determined by the Program Chair and as approved by the Dean.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: As a BulSU policy, the duration when the student is on official leave may not be counted against his/her allotted free tuition as provided by the UNIFAST ruling.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Returning students who did not file a leave of absence prior to discontinuing their studies for a period of not more than six semesters must submit a letter requesting to be readmitted to the program explaining the reason for their absence.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: They may be considered for readmission depending on their previous academic performance and the availability of slots in the program as determined by the Dean.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: 2.2 If the slot is available, the students may be advised to return to his/her program of study after submitting the following documents: 2.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: When readmitted, the returning students may be allowed to continue their old curriculum if they are incoming third year/higher year level otherwise, they will con tinue under the new curriculum existing at the time of their re -admission or whichever is the most expedient way for the students to finish their program as determined by the Program Chair and as approved by the Dean.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: They will be advised to apply for cred its of taken courses such as General Education (GE) subjects, Physical Education (PE), National Service Training Program (NSTP).
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Returning students who did not file a leave of absence may still avail of the free tuition under UNIFAST provided that t he semesters they were on unofficial leave will be counted against the allotted free tuition based on the allotted number of years of their academic program plus one -year grace period.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Admission Guidelines for Second Undergraduate Degree Takers4 The following guidelines for undergraduate degree programs at BulSU may apply to students who wish to take their second undergraduate degree program at the university: 1.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: The Second Undergraduate Degree Takers may apply to their preferred degree program subje ct to the availability of slots.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Follow the procedures provided @ iadmissions.bulsu.edu.ph and prepare the following documents for submission: 2.1.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Application form with two (2) identical pieces of 2”x 2” colored ID picture with white background and a n ame tag 2.2.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Marriage Certificate (photocopy) for married females only if there has been a change in surname from what is reflected on the transcript of record/diploma.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: PSA Birth Certificate (Photoc opy) 2.6 Letter of Intent Addressed to the Dean of the College bearing the reason why he /she wishes to take a second undergraduate degree program.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: GE courses taken in the first undergraduate degree may be credited provided that the number of units to be credited does not exceed 50% of the Units required by the second program at BulSU.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: 3.1 applicable GE subjects taken in at BulSU and GE, PE and NSTP courses taken from another HEI with higher or equal accreditation level as BulSU will automatically credited 3.2 In the case of major subjects taken in BulSU (i.e, within the area of specialization), credit will be determined by the Dean of the College with the approval of the Registrar’s Office.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Applicants who have taken their First Undergraduate Degree in another HEI will have to undergo a validation examination for each subject that they are applying for credits.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: The s tudent must have answered correctly 75% of the questions for each examination.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Note: The ETEEAP or Expanded Tertiary Education, Equivalency and Accreditation Program may be used as the mode of assessment to those who will apply for the second undergraduat e degree because it allows individuals to attain an academic degree through an assessment process that recognizes, credits, and gives equivalencies to knowledge, skills, and prior learning gained from relevant formal, non-formal, and informal training and work experiences.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: Second Undergraduate Degree Takers cannot avail of Free Higher Education and shall be charged tuition and other school fees as determined by the Governing Boards of the University (Unifast,2018).
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: References: Bulacan State University - Undergrad uate Student Manual, BOR Resolution No.
chunks.title: BulSU-Enhanced-Guidelines

chunks.content: PIMENTEL HALL AND NSTP BUILDING FLOOR PLAN Pimentel Hall Pimentel Hall stands as one of the key academic buildings at Bulacan State University in Malolos City, Bulacan.
chunks.title: CICT Rooms - Pics & Desc.docx

chunks.content: Rosario Pimentel, the first president of the BSU, the building honors his legacy of leadership and service.
chunks.title: CICT Rooms - Pics & Desc.docx

chunks.content: It houses the College of Nursing (CON) and the College of Information and Communications Technology (CICT)—with the first floor shared by both colleges, the second floor occupied by CON, and the third and fourth floors serving as the home of CICT.
chunks.title: CICT Rooms - Pics & Desc.docx

chunks.content: Its modern design provides a dynamic environment for classes, seminars, and hands-on learning.
chunks.title: CICT Rooms - Pics & Desc.docx

chunks.content: With its strategic location and well-designed structure, Pimentel Hall continues to promote academic excellence, innovation, and collaboration among BulSU students.
chunks.title: CICT Rooms - Pics & Desc.docx
